1→# AI MODEL SELECTION: Privacy & Threat Analysis (December 2025)
     2→
     3→**Date:** December 8, 2025  
     4→**CORRECTION:** Analysis updated with actual December 2025 models  
     5→**Context:** Common Cloud deployment for vulnerable communities (NSL/subpoena threat model)
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## CRITICAL ERROR ACKNOWLEDGED
    10→
    11→**I incorrectly cited Claude 3.7 Sonnet when the following models are actually available as of December 2025:**
    12→
    13→- **Claude Opus 4.5** (released Nov 23, 2025)
    14→- **Claude Sonnet 4.5** (released Sept 2025, updated Nov 2025)
    15→- **Claude Haiku 4.5** (released Oct 2025)
    16→- **OpenAI GPT-5.1** (released Nov 11, 2025)
    17→- **OpenAI GPT-5.1-Codex-Max** (released Nov 19, 2025)
    18→- **OpenAI o3 / o4-mini** (released Apr 2025)
    19→- **Gemini 3.0 Pro** (released Nov 18, 2025)
    20→- **DeepSeek-V3.2** (released Dec 1, 2025)
    21→
    22→---
    23→
    24→## PART 1: ACTUAL DECEMBER 2025 MODEL LANDSCAPE
    25→
    26→### **OpenAI GPT-5.1 / GPT-5.1-Codex-Max**
    27→
    28→**Release Dates:**
    29→- GPT-5.1: November 11, 2025
    30→- GPT-5.1-Codex-Max: November 19, 2025
    31→- GPT-5.2: Expected December 9, 2025 (per The Verge leak)
    32→
    33→**Key Features:**
    34→- **GPT-5.1 Instant:** "Warmer, more intelligent, better at following instructions"
    35→- **GPT-5.1 Thinking:** "Easier to understand, faster on simple tasks, more persistent on complex ones"
    36→- **GPT-5.1-Codex-Max:** Multi-context window processing ("compaction"), autonomous coding agents that work for hours/days
    37→- **Personalization:** Updated styles and tone options, custom instructions apply across all chats immediately
    38→
    39→**Performance:**
    40→- SWE-bench: ~62-65% (estimated, not officially published yet)
    41→- Real-world: 45+ minute tasks in single pass (per GPT-5.1 release notes)
    42→
    43→**Cost:**
    44→- ChatGPT Pro: $200/mo (includes GPT-5.1 Pro)
    45→- API: ~$10-30/M tokens (exact pricing TBD for GPT-5.1-Codex-Max)
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→### **Claude Opus 4.5 / Sonnet 4.5 / Haiku 4.5**
    50→
    51→**Release Dates:**
    52→- Sonnet 4.5: September 2025
    53→- Haiku 4.5: October 2025
    54→- Opus 4.5: November 23, 2025
    55→
    56→**Key Features (Opus 4.5):**
    57→- **Best-in-class coding:** State-of-the-art Terminal Bench (15% improvement over Sonnet 4.5)
    58→- **Effort parameter:** Control token budget for thoroughness vs efficiency tradeoff
    59→- **Extended thinking:** Preserves all reasoning blocks across conversations
    60→- **Communication style:** Concise, direct, natural (skips verbose summaries unless prompted)
    61→- **Context:** 200K tokens standard, 1M tokens (beta)
    62→
    63→**Performance:**
    64→- Terminal Bench: +15% over Sonnet 4.5
    65→- Coding: "Handles ambiguity and reasons about tradeoffs without hand-holding" (Anthropic internal testing)
    66→- Fewer steps to solve tasks = fewer tokens used (more precise)
    67→
    68→**Cost:**
    69→- Opus 4.5: "More accessible price point than previous Opus models" (exact pricing not disclosed, but reportedly competitive with GPT-5)
    70→- Sonnet 4.5: $3/M input, $15/M output
    71→- Haiku 4.5: Fastest, lowest cost
    72→
    73→**Availability:**
    74→- Claude.ai (Pro, Max, Team Premium users)
    75→- Claude Code (Pro users)
    76→- API (anthropic.com)
    77→- Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI
    78→
    79→---
    80→
    81→### **Gemini 3.0 Pro**
    82→
    83→**Release Date:** November 18, 2025
    84→
    85→**Key Features:**
    86→- **State-of-the-art reasoning:** #1 on LM Arena leaderboards (per Google blog)
    87→- **Multimodal:** "Best in the world for multimodal understanding" (text, images, audio, video)
    88→- **Agentic coding:** "Our most powerful agentic and vibe coding model yet"
    89→- **Gemini 3 Deep Think:** Enhanced reasoning mode (Google AI Ultra subscribers)
    90→- **Context:** 1M tokens (same as Gemini 2.x)
    91→
    92→**Performance:**
    93→- LM Arena: #1 position (beating GPT-5, Claude Opus 4.5 at time of release)
    94→- Multimodal: Outperforms GPT-5 and Claude on image/video understanding tasks
    95→
    96→**Cost:**
    97→- Free tier: 60 requests/minute
    98→- Paid: ~$7/M tokens (slightly above previous Gemini 2.5 Pro pricing)
    99→
   100→**Availability:**
   101→- Gemini app (all users, higher limits for Plus/Pro/Ultra)
   102→- Google AI Studio (free)
   103→- Vertex AI (GCP)
   104→
   105→---
   106→
   107→### **DeepSeek-V3.2 / V3.2-Speciale**
   108→
   109→**Release Date:** December 1, 2025
   110→
   111→**Key Features:**
   112→- **Open-source:** Matches GPT-5/Gemini 3.0 Pro at 89x lower cost
   113→- **671B parameters:** 37B active per token (MoE architecture)
   114→- **DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA):** 50% cost reduction for long contexts
   115→- **V3.2-Speciale:** Maxed-out reasoning (temporary model, expires Dec 15, 2025)
   116→
   117→**Performance:**
   118→- AIME 2025 (math): 96.0% pass rate (beats GPT-5's 94.6%!)
   119→- Gold-medal level: IMO, CMO, ICPC, IOI competitions
   120→- Coding: State-of-the-art on tool-use benchmarks
   121→
   122→**Cost:**
   123→- **$0.028/M input tokens** (vs GPT-4o at $2.50/M = 89x cheaper!)
   124→- **$1.10/M output tokens**
   125→- Self-hostable (open-source weights)
   126→
   127→---
   128→
   129→## PART 2: PRIVACY & THREAT ANALYSIS BY PROVIDER
   130→
   131→### **CRITICAL FINDING: ALL CHAT HISTORY CAN BE SUBPOENAED**
   132→
   133→**Recent Court Rulings (2025):**
   134→
   135→1. **NYT v. OpenAI (June 2025):** Federal judge ordered OpenAI to preserve ALL ChatGPT user logs, including "temporary" chats and API requests, regardless of user opt-out settings[230][242]
   136→   - Result: "We do not retain your chats" promise overridden by court order
   137→   - All business logs (trade secrets, privileged data) must be preserved
   138→
   139→2. **Divorce/Custody Cases (2024-2025):** Multiple cases used ChatGPT/Claude history as evidence[224][227][233]
   140→   - Custody case: ChatGPT history questioned parent's mental fitness
   141→   - Workplace dispute: Private conversations proved harassment
   142→   - Divorce case: Spouse's AI chats subpoenaed for hidden assets
   143→
   144→3. **Legal Precedent:** Courts treat AI chat history like any digital evidence (texts, emails)[224][236]
   145→   - Admissible in court
   146→   - No attorney-client privilege for AI conversations
   147→   - Deletion after preservation notice = spoliation (sanctions)
   148→
   149→---
   150→
   151→### **OpenAI (ChatGPT / GPT-5.1) Privacy Analysis**
   152→
   153→#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**
   154→
   155→**ChatGPT Plus/Pro ($20-200/mo):**
   156→- **Default:** Chats saved for training unless you disable chat history
   157→- **"Temporary" chats:** Previously deleted after session, BUT NYT court order requires preservation regardless of settings[230][242]
   158→- **Opt-out:** Settings → Data Controls → "Improve the model for everyone" (toggle off)
   159→  - Effect: Data not used for training
   160→  - BUT: Still retained for 30 days minimum (compliance, abuse detection)
   161→  - AND: Court can still subpoena (retention override)
   162→
   163→**ChatGPT API / Business:**
   164→- **Zero data retention agreement (ZDR):** Available for Enterprise customers
   165→- **Default API:** 30-day retention for abuse/monitoring
   166→- **BUT:** NYT ruling suggests courts can compel preservation regardless of ZDR
   167→
   168→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   169→
   170→**How to Opt Out:**
   171→1. Go to Settings → Data Controls
   172→2. Toggle OFF: "Improve the model for everyone"
   173→3. Toggle ON: "Temporary Chat" (but see court order caveat)
   174→
   175→**Effect:**
   176→- ✅ Your chats won't train GPT-6
   177→- ⚠️ Still retained 30 days (abuse monitoring)
   178→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   183→
   184→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   185→|----------|-----------|-----|
   186→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | FBI can serve NSL directly to OpenAI; gag order prevents disclosure; full chat history accessible |
   187→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Texas/AZ AG can subpoena OpenAI (US company, US jurisdiction); immediate compliance likely |
   188→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | OpenAI is US-based; cross-border transfer concerns; GDPR right to deletion may conflict with US court orders |
   189→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Multiple precedents; courts routinely subpoena ChatGPT history[227][233][236] |
   190→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer can request logs if using company API keys; individual use can be subpoenaed in disputes |
   191→
   192→**Key Risk:** OpenAI is **US-based (California)**, subject to US courts, Five Eyes intelligence sharing, and NSL/FISA court orders[224][230][242].
   193→
   194→---
   195→
   196→### **Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.5 / Sonnet 4.5) Privacy Analysis**
   197→
   198→#### **Data Retention Policy (Updated Sept 28, 2025)**
   199→
   200→**MAJOR POLICY CHANGE (Sept 2025):**
   201→- **Previous:** 30-day retention (all users)
   202→- **New (as of Sept 28, 2025):** **5-year retention** if you allow training[225][228][234][237][240]
   203→
   204→**Consumer Accounts (Free, Pro, Max):**
   205→- **Default:** "Help improve Claude" toggle = **ON** (opt-in to training)
   206→- **If enabled:** 5-year retention, data used for model training
   207→- **If disabled:** 30-day retention, no training use
   208→- **Critical:** Team/Max users ($18-100+/mo) are treated as "consumers" unless on Commercial Terms[225]
   209→
   210→**Business/Enterprise (Claude for Work, API):**
   211→- **Commercial Terms:** No training, minimal retention, GDPR-compliant
   212→- **Zero Data Retention (ZDR):** Available for API users (but see caveat below)
   213→- **BUT:** ZDR doesn't cover Files API exception[228]
   214→
   215→#### **The 5-Year Retention Controversy**
   216→
   217→**Why This Matters:**
   218→- **6,000% increase** from 30 days to 5 years (1,825 days)[225]
   219→- Your 2025 conversations could train Claude models in 2030
   220→- Law firms, consultants, healthcare providers potentially violating compliance (HIPAA, attorney-client privilege)[225]
   221→- **No middle option:** Either allow training + 5 years, OR disable training + lose personalization features[231]
   222→
   223→**Affected Users:**
   224→- ✅ All Claude Free users
   225→- ✅ All Claude Pro users ($20/mo)
   226→- ✅ All Claude Max users ($100+/mo)
   227→- ✅ All Claude Team users (unless upgraded to Enterprise)
   228→- ❌ Claude for Work / Enterprise (Commercial Terms apply)
   229→- ❌ API users with ZDR agreement
   230→
   231→---
   232→
   233→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   234→
   235→**How to Opt Out:**
   236→1. Go to Settings → Privacy Settings
   237→2. Toggle OFF: "Help improve Claude"
   238→3. File Data Processing Objection (optional, for formal record)
   239→
   240→**Effect:**
   241→- ✅ Data not used for training
   242→- ✅ Retention reduced to 30 days
   243→- ❌ Lose personalization/memory features
   244→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
   245→
   246→**Trust & Safety Exception:**
   247→- Content flagged for abuse retained up to **2 years** (inputs/outputs)
   248→- Classification scores retained up to **7 years**
   249→- Applies even if training disabled[228]
   250→
   251→---
   252→
   253→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   254→
   255→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   256→|----------|-----------|-----|
   257→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Anthropic is US-based (California); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; 5-year retention = extended exposure |
   258→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction, immediate compliance likely; 5-year retention means old conversations still accessible |
   259→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | US-based company, cross-border transfer concerns; GDPR deletion rights may conflict with 5-year retention |
   260→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | No precedent yet, but same risk as ChatGPT; courts will treat Claude history like ChatGPT[224][227][239] |
   261→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer API keys = employer can request logs; individual use can be subpoenaed |
   262→
   263→**Key Risk:** Anthropic is **US-based (San Francisco)**, subject to same legal framework as OpenAI[225][234][237][239].
   264→
   265→**Additional Privacy Concern:** 5-year retention policy means conversations from 2020 still accessible in 2025 (if you had training enabled)[225][240].
   266→
   267→---
   268→
   269→### **Google (Gemini 3.0 Pro) Privacy Analysis**
   270→
   271→#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**
   272→
   273→**Gemini App (Free, Plus, Pro, Ultra):**
   274→- **Default:** Conversations saved for training unless you disable
   275→- **Opt-out:** Settings → Gemini Apps Activity → "Save Gemini Apps Activity" (toggle off)
   276→  - Effect: Conversations not saved, not used for training
   277→  - BUT: Google can still log metadata (timestamps, query types) for abuse detection
   278→
   279→**Gemini API / Vertex AI (Business):**
   280→- **ZDR available:** API users can opt out of data retention
   281→- **Grounding with Google Search:** Billing starts Jan 5, 2026 (previously free)[207]
   282→
   283→**Google Workspace (Business):**
   284→- **Smart features disabled by default:** Admins must enable for AI training
   285→- **Can opt out:** Settings → "Use my data for AI improvement" (toggle off)
   286→
   287→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   288→
   289→**How to Opt Out (Gemini App):**
   290→1. Go to Gemini Apps Activity settings
   291→2. Toggle OFF: "Save Gemini Apps Activity"
   292→3. Delete existing history (optional)
   293→
   294→**How to Opt Out (Workspace):**
   295→1. Admin console → Settings → Smart features
   296→2. Disable: "Use data for AI training"
   297→
   298→**Effect:**
   299→- ✅ Conversations not used for training
   300→- ⚠️ Metadata still logged (abuse/compliance)
   301→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
   302→
   303→---
   304→
   305→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   306→
   307→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   308→|----------|-----------|-----|
   309→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Google is US-based (California); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; extensive data retention infrastructure |
   310→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction; Google has cooperated with law enforcement in past; immediate compliance likely |
   311→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **MODERATE** | Google has EU data centers, GDPR-compliant infrastructure; better than OpenAI/Anthropic but still US parent company |
   312→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Same risk as ChatGPT/Claude; courts will subpoena Gemini history |
   313→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Google Workspace admins can access user logs; individual Gemini use can be subpoenaed |
   314→
   315→**Key Risk:** Google is **US-based (California)**, but has **extensive EU infrastructure** (Gemini available via Vertex AI in EU regions)[213][216].
   316→
   317→**Advantage over OpenAI/Anthropic:** Google has EU data centers, can route EU traffic through EU-only infrastructure for better GDPR compliance.
   318→
   319→---
   320→
   321→### **DeepSeek (V3.2) Privacy Analysis**
   322→
   323→#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**
   324→
   325→**DeepSeek API:**
   326→- **China-based company:** Subject to Chinese data protection laws
   327→- **Data retention:** Not clearly disclosed (no public privacy policy in English as of Dec 2025)
   328→- **Training use:** Assumed data used for training (standard for Chinese AI companies)
   329→
   330→**Self-Hosted (Open-Source):**
   331→- **You control data:** Run on your own servers
   332→- **No external transmission:** Data never leaves your infrastructure
   333→- **Zero retention risk:** No company can be subpoenaed if you self-host
   334→
   335→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   336→
   337→**DeepSeek API:**
   338→- **No opt-out disclosed:** Assume all data used for training
   339→- **Recommendation:** DO NOT use DeepSeek API for sensitive data
   340→
   341→**Self-Hosted:**
   342→- **N/A:** You control all data
   343→
   344→---
   345→
   346→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   347→
   348→| Scenario | Risk Level | Explanation |
   349→|----------|-----------|-------------|
   350→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | ⚠️ **COMPLICATED** | China-based company; US courts have no jurisdiction; BUT Chinese gov't can access data under China's National Security Law |
   351→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🟢 **LOW** | US courts cannot compel Chinese company; Texas AG has no jurisdiction |
   352→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | ⚠️ **COMPLICATED** | No EU presence; GDPR doesn't apply; but Chinese data protection laws may apply |
   353→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🟢 **LOW** | US courts unlikely to successfully subpoena Chinese company |
   354→| **Chinese Gov't Access** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Chinese National Security Law requires companies to provide data to gov't on request (no judicial oversight) |
   355→
   356→**Key Risk:** DeepSeek is **China-based (Beijing)**, subject to Chinese government data access laws[177][183][186][192].
   357→
   358→**Recommendation for Vulnerable Communities:** **DO NOT use DeepSeek API** if serving populations at risk from Chinese government (Uyghurs, Hong Kong activists, Taiwan-related orgs, etc.).
   359→
   360→**Safe Alternative:** **Self-host DeepSeek** (open-source weights) on your own servers (Iceland, Switzerland) → zero external data transmission.
   361→
   362→---
   363→
   364→## PART 3: PRIVACY SCORECARD & RECOMMENDATIONS
   365→
   366→### **Privacy Risk Matrix (December 2025)**
   367→
   368→| Provider | Model | US Jurisdiction | Data Retention | Training Opt-Out | Subpoena Risk | Court Precedent | **Overall Risk** |
   369→|----------|-------|----------------|----------------|-----------------|---------------|----------------|------------------|
   370→| **OpenAI** | GPT-5.1 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | 30+ days | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ✅ Yes (NYT case) | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
   371→| **Anthropic** | Opus 4.5 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | 5 years (!) | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ⚠️ Likely same | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
   372→| **Google** | Gemini 3.0 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | Varies | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ⚠️ Likely same | **🔴 HIGH** |
   373→| **DeepSeek** | V3.2 (API) | 🟡 China | Unknown | ❌ None | ⚠️ China gov't | ❌ None | **🔴 HIGH** |
   374→| **DeepSeek** | V3.2 (Self-Host) | ✅ Your choice | ✅ You control | ✅ N/A | 🟢 None | ❌ None | **🟢 LOWEST** |
   375→
   376→---
   377→
   378→### **RECOMMENDATIONS FOR COMMON CLOUD DEPLOYMENT**
   379→
   380→#### **For MVP (Months 1-6): Use API with Maximum Privacy Settings**
   381→
   382→**Option 1: Claude Opus 4.5 API (Recommended)**
   383→- ✅ Commercial Terms (no training, minimal retention)
   384→- ✅ Best coding performance (Terminal Bench leader)
   385→- ✅ Extended thinking (preserves reasoning across conversations)
   386→- ❌ US jurisdiction (NSL risk)
   387→- ❌ 5-year retention if using consumer account
   388→
   389→**Setup:**
   390→```
   391→1. Sign up for Claude API (Commercial Terms, NOT consumer)
   392→2. Disable all training/logging: API settings → "Zero Data Retention"
   393→3. Use API keys only (never Claude.ai consumer interface)
   394→4. Rotate API keys monthly (minimize exposure window)
   395→5. Log all prompts locally (your infrastructure, encrypted)
   396→6. Never use consumer Claude.ai for deployment work
   397→```
   398→
   399→**Cost:** ~$3-15/M tokens (Opus 4.5 pricing TBD, likely competitive with GPT-5)
   400→
   401→---
   402→
   403→**Option 2: Self-Hosted DeepSeek-V3.2 (Maximum Privacy)**
   404→- ✅ You control all data (Iceland/Switzerland servers)
   405→- ✅ Zero external transmission
   406→- ✅ No company can be subpoenaed
   407→- ✅ Open-source (auditable)
   408→- ❌ Requires significant compute (8x A100 GPUs or equivalent)
   409→- ❌ Complex setup (not viable for solo operator)
   410→
   411→**Setup:**
   412→```
   413→1. Provision GPU server in Iceland (Hosting.is) or Switzerland (Infomaniak)
   414→2. Download DeepSeek-V3.2 weights (HuggingFace)
   415→3. Install vLLM or TGI (inference server)
   416→4. Deploy n8n → calls local DeepSeek instance
   417→5. All data stays on your servers
   418→6. No external API calls = zero subpoena risk
   419→```
   420→
   421→**Cost:** 
   422→- GPU server: €500-1000/mo (8x A100 equivalent)
   423→- One-time setup: ~€5,000 (engineer time)
   424→- **NOT RECOMMENDED for MVP** (too expensive/complex)
   425→
   426→---
   427→
   428→**Option 3: Gemini 3.0 Pro API via Vertex AI (EU-Compliant Compromise)**
   429→- ✅ EU data centers (Germany, Netherlands)
   430→- ✅ GDPR-compliant infrastructure
   431→- ⚠️ Still US parent company (Google)
   432→- ⚠️ Can be subpoenaed by US courts (but harder than OpenAI/Anthropic)
   433→
   434→**Setup:**
   435→```
   436→1. Sign up for Google Cloud (Vertex AI)
   437→2. Select EU region (europe-west1 = Belgium, europe-west3 = Frankfurt)
   438→3. Enable Gemini 3.0 Pro API
   439→4. Configure: Data residency = EU only
   440→5. Use Vertex AI API (NOT Gemini.ai consumer interface)
   441→6. Rotate API keys monthly
   442→```
   443→
   444→**Cost:** ~$7/M tokens (slightly higher than Claude/GPT)
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→### **CRITICAL: NEVER Use Consumer Interfaces for Deployment**
   449→
   450→**❌ DO NOT USE:**
   451→- ChatGPT.com (consumer interface)
   452→- Claude.ai (consumer interface)
   453→- Gemini app (consumer interface)
   454→
   455→**✅ ALWAYS USE:**
   456→- OpenAI API (with ZDR agreement)
   457→- Claude API (Commercial Terms)
   458→- Vertex AI (Gemini API)
   459→
   460→**Why:** Consumer interfaces have **no privacy guarantees**, **5-year retention** (Claude), and **can be subpoenaed** (NYT precedent)[225][230][234][242].
   461→
   462→---
   463→
   464→## PART 4: UPDATED AI TOOLCHAIN (DECEMBER 2025)
   465→
   466→### **The Privacy-First Stack**
   467→
   468→```
   469→PLANNING & ARCHITECTURE (10% human, 90% AI)
   470→├─ Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)
   471→│   └─ Prompt: "Design 3-region DBA architecture"
   472→│   └─ Output: 50-page spec with Terraform modules
   473→│   └─ Privacy: Commercial Terms, minimal retention, no training
   474→│
   475→CODING & CONFIGURATION (5% human, 95% AI)
   476→├─ Cursor AI (with Claude Opus 4.5 API)
   477→│   └─ Configure: Use API key (not Claude.ai consumer)
   478→│   └─ Multi-file editing: Ansible playbooks, Docker configs
   479→│   └─ Privacy: All data via API (Commercial Terms)
   480→│
   481→WORKFLOW ORCHESTRATION (0% human, 100% AI)
   482→├─ n8n AI Workflow Builder

   506→

   538→

# AI MODEL SELECTION: Privacy & Threat Analysis (December 2025)

**Date:** December 8, 2025  
**CORRECTION:** Analysis updated with actual December 2025 models  
**Context:** Common Cloud deployment for vulnerable communities (NSL/subpoena threat model)

---

## CRITICAL ERROR ACKNOWLEDGED

**I incorrectly cited Claude 3.7 Sonnet when the following models are actually available as of December 2025:**

- **Claude Opus 4.5** (released Nov 23, 2025)
- **Claude Sonnet 4.5** (released Sept 2025, updated Nov 2025)
- **Claude Haiku 4.5** (released Oct 2025)
- **OpenAI GPT-5.1** (released Nov 11, 2025)
- **OpenAI GPT-5.1-Codex-Max** (released Nov 19, 2025)
- **OpenAI o3 / o4-mini** (released Apr 2025)
- **Gemini 3.0 Pro** (released Nov 18, 2025)
- **DeepSeek-V3.2** (released Dec 1, 2025)

---

## PART 1: ACTUAL DECEMBER 2025 MODEL LANDSCAPE

### **OpenAI GPT-5.1 / GPT-5.1-Codex-Max**

**Release Dates:**
- GPT-5.1: November 11, 2025
- GPT-5.1-Codex-Max: November 19, 2025
- GPT-5.2: Expected December 9, 2025 (per The Verge leak)

**Key Features:**
- **GPT-5.1 Instant:** "Warmer, more intelligent, better at following instructions"
- **GPT-5.1 Thinking:** "Easier to understand, faster on simple tasks, more persistent on complex ones"
- **GPT-5.1-Codex-Max:** Multi-context window processing ("compaction"), autonomous coding agents that work for hours/days
- **Personalization:** Updated styles and tone options, custom instructions apply across all chats immediately

**Performance:**
- SWE-bench: ~62-65% (estimated, not officially published yet)
- Real-world: 45+ minute tasks in single pass (per GPT-5.1 release notes)

**Cost:**
- ChatGPT Pro: $200/mo (includes GPT-5.1 Pro)
- API: ~$10-30/M tokens (exact pricing TBD for GPT-5.1-Codex-Max)

---

### **Claude Opus 4.5 / Sonnet 4.5 / Haiku 4.5**

**Release Dates:**
- Sonnet 4.5: September 2025
- Haiku 4.5: October 2025
- Opus 4.5: November 23, 2025

**Key Features (Opus 4.5):**
- **Best-in-class coding:** State-of-the-art Terminal Bench (15% improvement over Sonnet 4.5)
- **Effort parameter:** Control token budget for thoroughness vs efficiency tradeoff
- **Extended thinking:** Preserves all reasoning blocks across conversations
- **Communication style:** Concise, direct, natural (skips verbose summaries unless prompted)
- **Context:** 200K tokens standard, 1M tokens (beta)

**Performance:**
- Terminal Bench: +15% over Sonnet 4.5
- Coding: "Handles ambiguity and reasons about tradeoffs without hand-holding" (Anthropic internal testing)
- Fewer steps to solve tasks = fewer tokens used (more precise)

**Cost:**
- Opus 4.5: "More accessible price point than previous Opus models" (exact pricing not disclosed, but reportedly competitive with GPT-5)
- Sonnet 4.5: $3/M input, $15/M output
- Haiku 4.5: Fastest, lowest cost

**Availability:**
- Claude.ai (Pro, Max, Team Premium users)
- Claude Code (Pro users)
- API (anthropic.com)
- Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI

---

### **Gemini 3.0 Pro**

**Release Date:** November 18, 2025

**Key Features:**
- **State-of-the-art reasoning:** #1 on LM Arena leaderboards (per Google blog)
- **Multimodal:** "Best in the world for multimodal understanding" (text, images, audio, video)
- **Agentic coding:** "Our most powerful agentic and vibe coding model yet"
- **Gemini 3 Deep Think:** Enhanced reasoning mode (Google AI Ultra subscribers)
- **Context:** 1M tokens (same as Gemini 2.x)

**Performance:**
- LM Arena: #1 position (beating GPT-5, Claude Opus 4.5 at time of release)
- Multimodal: Outperforms GPT-5 and Claude on image/video understanding tasks

**Cost:**
- Free tier: 60 requests/minute
- Paid: ~$7/M tokens (slightly above previous Gemini 2.5 Pro pricing)

**Availability:**
- Gemini app (all users, higher limits for Plus/Pro/Ultra)
- Google AI Studio (free)
- Vertex AI (GCP)

---

### **DeepSeek-V3.2 / V3.2-Speciale**

**Release Date:** December 1, 2025

**Key Features:**
- **Open-source:** Matches GPT-5/Gemini 3.0 Pro at 89x lower cost
- **671B parameters:** 37B active per token (MoE architecture)
- **DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA):** 50% cost reduction for long contexts
- **V3.2-Speciale:** Maxed-out reasoning (temporary model, expires Dec 15, 2025)

**Performance:**
- AIME 2025 (math): 96.0% pass rate (beats GPT-5's 94.6%!)
- Gold-medal level: IMO, CMO, ICPC, IOI competitions
- Coding: State-of-the-art on tool-use benchmarks

**Cost:**
- **$0.028/M input tokens** (vs GPT-4o at $2.50/M = 89x cheaper!)
- **$1.10/M output tokens**
- Self-hostable (open-source weights)

---

## PART 2: PRIVACY & THREAT ANALYSIS BY PROVIDER

### **CRITICAL FINDING: ALL CHAT HISTORY CAN BE SUBPOENAED**

**Recent Court Rulings (2025):**

1. **NYT v. OpenAI (June 2025):** Federal judge ordered OpenAI to preserve ALL ChatGPT user logs, including "temporary" chats and API requests, regardless of user opt-out settings[230][242]
   - Result: "We do not retain your chats" promise overridden by court order
   - All business logs (trade secrets, privileged data) must be preserved

2. **Divorce/Custody Cases (2024-2025):** Multiple cases used ChatGPT/Claude history as evidence[224][227][233]
   - Custody case: ChatGPT history questioned parent's mental fitness
   - Workplace dispute: Private conversations proved harassment
   - Divorce case: Spouse's AI chats subpoenaed for hidden assets

3. **Legal Precedent:** Courts treat AI chat history like any digital evidence (texts, emails)[224][236]
   - Admissible in court
   - No attorney-client privilege for AI conversations
   - Deletion after preservation notice = spoliation (sanctions)

---

### **OpenAI (ChatGPT / GPT-5.1) Privacy Analysis**

#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**

**ChatGPT Plus/Pro ($20-200/mo):**
- **Default:** Chats saved for training unless you disable chat history
- **"Temporary" chats:** Previously deleted after session, BUT NYT court order requires preservation regardless of settings[230][242]
- **Opt-out:** Settings → Data Controls → "Improve the model for everyone" (toggle off)
  - Effect: Data not used for training
  - BUT: Still retained for 30 days minimum (compliance, abuse detection)
  - AND: Court can still subpoena (retention override)

**ChatGPT API / Business:**
- **Zero data retention agreement (ZDR):** Available for Enterprise customers
- **Default API:** 30-day retention for abuse/monitoring
- **BUT:** NYT ruling suggests courts can compel preservation regardless of ZDR

#### **Training Data Opt-Out**

**How to Opt Out:**
1. Go to Settings → Data Controls
2. Toggle OFF: "Improve the model for everyone"
3. Toggle ON: "Temporary Chat" (but see court order caveat)

**Effect:**
- ✅ Your chats won't train GPT-6
- ⚠️ Still retained 30 days (abuse monitoring)
- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)

---

#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**

| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
|----------|-----------|-----|
| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | FBI can serve NSL directly to OpenAI; gag order prevents disclosure; full chat history accessible |
| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Texas/AZ AG can subpoena OpenAI (US company, US jurisdiction); immediate compliance likely |
| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | OpenAI is US-based; cross-border transfer concerns; GDPR right to deletion may conflict with US court orders |
| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Multiple precedents; courts routinely subpoena ChatGPT history[227][233][236] |
| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer can request logs if using company API keys; individual use can be subpoenaed in disputes |

**Key Risk:** OpenAI is **US-based (California)**, subject to US courts, Five Eyes intelligence sharing, and NSL/FISA court orders[224][230][242].

---

### **Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.5 / Sonnet 4.5) Privacy Analysis**

#### **Data Retention Policy (Updated Sept 28, 2025)**

**MAJOR POLICY CHANGE (Sept 2025):**
- **Previous:** 30-day retention (all users)
- **New (as of Sept 28, 2025):** **5-year retention** if you allow training[225][228][234][237][240]

**Consumer Accounts (Free, Pro, Max):**
- **Default:** "Help improve Claude" toggle = **ON** (opt-in to training)
- **If enabled:** 5-year retention, data used for model training
- **If disabled:** 30-day retention, no training use
- **Critical:** Team/Max users ($18-100+/mo) are treated as "consumers" unless on Commercial Terms[225]

**Business/Enterprise (Claude for Work, API):**
- **Commercial Terms:** No training, minimal retention, GDPR-compliant
- **Zero Data Retention (ZDR):** Available for API users (but see caveat below)
- **BUT:** ZDR doesn't cover Files API exception[228]

#### **The 5-Year Retention Controversy**

**Why This Matters:**
- **6,000% increase** from 30 days to 5 years (1,825 days)[225]
- Your 2025 conversations could train Claude models in 2030
- Law firms, consultants, healthcare providers potentially violating compliance (HIPAA, attorney-client privilege)[225]
- **No middle option:** Either allow training + 5 years, OR disable training + lose personalization features[231]

**Affected Users:**
- ✅ All Claude Free users
- ✅ All Claude Pro users ($20/mo)
- ✅ All Claude Max users ($100+/mo)
- ✅ All Claude Team users (unless upgraded to Enterprise)
- ❌ Claude for Work / Enterprise (Commercial Terms apply)
- ❌ API users with ZDR agreement

---

#### **Training Data Opt-Out**

**How to Opt Out:**
1. Go to Settings → Privacy Settings
2. Toggle OFF: "Help improve Claude"
3. File Data Processing Objection (optional, for formal record)

**Effect:**
- ✅ Data not used for training
- ✅ Retention reduced to 30 days
- ❌ Lose personalization/memory features
- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)

**Trust & Safety Exception:**
- Content flagged for abuse retained up to **2 years** (inputs/outputs)
- Classification scores retained up to **7 years**
- Applies even if training disabled[228]

---

#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**

| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
|----------|-----------|-----|
| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Anthropic is US-based (California); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; 5-year retention = extended exposure |
| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction, immediate compliance likely; 5-year retention means old conversations still accessible |
| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | US-based company, cross-border transfer concerns; GDPR deletion rights may conflict with 5-year retention |
| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | No precedent yet, but same risk as ChatGPT; courts will treat Claude history like ChatGPT[224][227][239] |
| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer API keys = employer can request logs; individual use can be subpoenaed |

**Key Risk:** Anthropic is **US-based (San Francisco)**, subject to same legal framework as OpenAI[225][234][237][239].

**Additional Privacy Concern:** 5-year retention policy means conversations from 2020 still accessible in 2025 (if you had training enabled)[225][240].

---

### **Google (Gemini 3.0 Pro) Privacy Analysis**

#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**

**Gemini App (Free, Plus, Pro, Ultra):**
- **Default:** Conversations saved for training unless you disable
- **Opt-out:** Settings → Gemini Apps Activity → "Save Gemini Apps Activity" (toggle off)
  - Effect: Conversations not saved, not used for training
  - BUT: Google can still log metadata (timestamps, query types) for abuse detection

**Gemini API / Vertex AI (Business):**
- **ZDR available:** API users can opt out of data retention
- **Grounding with Google Search:** Billing starts Jan 5, 2026 (previously free)[207]

**Google Workspace (Business):**
- **Smart features disabled by default:** Admins must enable for AI training
- **Can opt out:** Settings → "Use my data for AI improvement" (toggle off)

#### **Training Data Opt-Out**

**How to Opt Out (Gemini App):**
1. Go to Gemini Apps Activity settings
2. Toggle OFF: "Save Gemini Apps Activity"
3. Delete existing history (optional)

**How to Opt Out (Workspace):**
1. Admin console → Settings → Smart features
2. Disable: "Use data for AI training"

**Effect:**
- ✅ Conversations not used for training
- ⚠️ Metadata still logged (abuse/compliance)
- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)

---

#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**

| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
|----------|-----------|-----|
| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Google is US-based (California); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; extensive data retention infrastructure |
| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction; Google has cooperated with law enforcement in past; immediate compliance likely |
| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **MODERATE** | Google has EU data centers, GDPR-compliant infrastructure; better than OpenAI/Anthropic but still US parent company |
| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Same risk as ChatGPT/Claude; courts will subpoena Gemini history |
| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Google Workspace admins can access user logs; individual Gemini use can be subpoenaed |

**Key Risk:** Google is **US-based (California)**, but has **extensive EU infrastructure** (Gemini available via Vertex AI in EU regions)[213][216].

**Advantage over OpenAI/Anthropic:** Google has EU data centers, can route EU traffic through EU-only infrastructure for better GDPR compliance.

---

### **DeepSeek (V3.2) Privacy Analysis**

#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**

**DeepSeek API:**
- **China-based company:** Subject to Chinese data protection laws
- **Data retention:** Not clearly disclosed (no public privacy policy in English as of Dec 2025)
- **Training use:** Assumed data used for training (standard for Chinese AI companies)

**Self-Hosted (Open-Source):**
- **You control data:** Run on your own servers
- **No external transmission:** Data never leaves your infrastructure
- **Zero retention risk:** No company can be subpoenaed if you self-host

#### **Training Data Opt-Out**

**DeepSeek API:**
- **No opt-out disclosed:** Assume all data used for training
- **Recommendation:** DO NOT use DeepSeek API for sensitive data

**Self-Hosted:**
- **N/A:** You control all data

---

#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**

| Scenario | Risk Level | Explanation |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | ⚠️ **COMPLICATED** | China-based company; US courts have no jurisdiction; BUT Chinese gov't can access data under China's National Security Law |
| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🟢 **LOW** | US courts cannot compel Chinese company; Texas AG has no jurisdiction |
| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | ⚠️ **COMPLICATED** | No EU presence; GDPR doesn't apply; but Chinese data protection laws may apply |
| **Divorce/Custody** | 🟢 **LOW** | US courts unlikely to successfully subpoena Chinese company |
| **Chinese Gov't Access** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Chinese National Security Law requires companies to provide data to gov't on request (no judicial oversight) |

**Key Risk:** DeepSeek is **China-based (Beijing)**, subject to Chinese government data access laws[177][183][186][192].

**Recommendation for Vulnerable Communities:** **DO NOT use DeepSeek API** if serving populations at risk from Chinese government (Uyghurs, Hong Kong activists, Taiwan-related orgs, etc.).

**Safe Alternative:** **Self-host DeepSeek** (open-source weights) on your own servers (Iceland, Switzerland) → zero external data transmission.

---

## PART 3: PRIVACY SCORECARD & RECOMMENDATIONS

### **Privacy Risk Matrix (December 2025)**

| Provider | Model | US Jurisdiction | Data Retention | Training Opt-Out | Subpoena Risk | Court Precedent | **Overall Risk** |
|----------|-------|----------------|----------------|-----------------|---------------|----------------|------------------|
| **OpenAI** | GPT-5.1 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | 30+ days | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ✅ Yes (NYT case) | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
| **Anthropic** | Opus 4.5 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | 5 years (!) | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ⚠️ Likely same | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
| **Google** | Gemini 3.0 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | Varies | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ⚠️ Likely same | **🔴 HIGH** |
| **DeepSeek** | V3.2 (API) | 🟡 China | Unknown | ❌ None | ⚠️ China gov't | ❌ None | **🔴 HIGH** |
| **DeepSeek** | V3.2 (Self-Host) | ✅ Your choice | ✅ You control | ✅ N/A | 🟢 None | ❌ None | **🟢 LOWEST** |

---

### **RECOMMENDATIONS FOR COMMON CLOUD DEPLOYMENT**

#### **For MVP (Months 1-6): Use API with Maximum Privacy Settings**

**Option 1: Claude Opus 4.5 API (Recommended)**
- ✅ Commercial Terms (no training, minimal retention)
- ✅ Best coding performance (Terminal Bench leader)
- ✅ Extended thinking (preserves reasoning across conversations)
- ❌ US jurisdiction (NSL risk)
- ❌ 5-year retention if using consumer account

**Setup:**
```
1. Sign up for Claude API (Commercial Terms, NOT consumer)
2. Disable all training/logging: API settings → "Zero Data Retention"
3. Use API keys only (never Claude.ai consumer interface)
4. Rotate API keys monthly (minimize exposure window)
5. Log all prompts locally (your infrastructure, encrypted)
6. Never use consumer Claude.ai for deployment work
```

**Cost:** ~$3-15/M tokens (Opus 4.5 pricing TBD, likely competitive with GPT-5)

---

**Option 2: Self-Hosted DeepSeek-V3.2 (Maximum Privacy)**
- ✅ You control all data (Iceland/Switzerland servers)
- ✅ Zero external transmission
- ✅ No company can be subpoenaed
- ✅ Open-source (auditable)
- ❌ Requires significant compute (8x A100 GPUs or equivalent)
- ❌ Complex setup (not viable for solo operator)

**Setup:**
```
1. Provision GPU server in Iceland (Hosting.is) or Switzerland (Infomaniak)
2. Download DeepSeek-V3.2 weights (HuggingFace)
3. Install vLLM or TGI (inference server)
4. Deploy n8n → calls local DeepSeek instance
5. All data stays on your servers
6. No external API calls = zero subpoena risk
```

**Cost:** 
- GPU server: €500-1000/mo (8x A100 equivalent)
- One-time setup: ~€5,000 (engineer time)
- **NOT RECOMMENDED for MVP** (too expensive/complex)

---

**Option 3: Gemini 3.0 Pro API via Vertex AI (EU-Compliant Compromise)**
- ✅ EU data centers (Germany, Netherlands)
- ✅ GDPR-compliant infrastructure
- ⚠️ Still US parent company (Google)
- ⚠️ Can be subpoenaed by US courts (but harder than OpenAI/Anthropic)

**Setup:**
```
1. Sign up for Google Cloud (Vertex AI)
2. Select EU region (europe-west1 = Belgium, europe-west3 = Frankfurt)
3. Enable Gemini 3.0 Pro API
4. Configure: Data residency = EU only
5. Use Vertex AI API (NOT Gemini.ai consumer interface)
6. Rotate API keys monthly
```

**Cost:** ~$7/M tokens (slightly higher than Claude/GPT)

---

### **CRITICAL: NEVER Use Consumer Interfaces for Deployment**

**❌ DO NOT USE:**
- ChatGPT.com (consumer interface)
- Claude.ai (consumer interface)
- Gemini app (consumer interface)

**✅ ALWAYS USE:**
- OpenAI API (with ZDR agreement)
- Claude API (Commercial Terms)
- Vertex AI (Gemini API)

**Why:** Consumer interfaces have **no privacy guarantees**, **5-year retention** (Claude), and **can be subpoenaed** (NYT precedent)[225][230][234][242].

---

## PART 4: UPDATED AI TOOLCHAIN (DECEMBER 2025)

### **The Privacy-First Stack**

```
PLANNING & ARCHITECTURE (10% human, 90% AI)
├─ Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)
│   └─ Prompt: "Design 3-region DBA architecture"
│   └─ Output: 50-page spec with Terraform modules
│   └─ Privacy: Commercial Terms, minimal retention, no training
│
CODING & CONFIGURATION (5% human, 95% AI)
├─ Cursor AI (with Claude Opus 4.5 API)
│   └─ Configure: Use API key (not Claude.ai consumer)
│   └─ Multi-file editing: Ansible playbooks, Docker configs
│   └─ Privacy: All data via API (Commercial Terms)
│
WORKFLOW ORCHESTRATION (0% human, 100% AI)
├─ n8n AI Workflow Builder
│   └─ Use Gemini 3.0 Pro (Vertex AI, EU region)
│   └─ OR: Self-hosted DeepSeek-V3.2 (if budget allows)
│   └─ Privacy: EU data residency OR self-hosted
│
TESTING & DEBUGGING (20% human, 80% AI)
├─ OpenAI o3-mini (API with ZDR)
│   └─ OR: Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)
│   └─ Privacy: ZDR agreement, minimal retention
│
MONITORING & INCIDENT RESPONSE (10% human, 90% AI)
├─ n8n + AI Agent monitoring workflows
│   └─ Use Gemini 3.0 Pro (Vertex AI, EU region)
│   └─ Privacy: EU data residency, no US API calls
│
DOCUMENTATION (0% human, 100% AI)
├─ Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)
│   └─ Generates deployment docs, runbooks, API refs
│   └─ Privacy: Commercial Terms, no training
```

---

## PART 5: COST COMPARISON (UPDATED DECEMBER 2025)

### **API Costs (per 1M tokens)**

| Model | Input Cost | Output Cost | Total (avg prompt) | Privacy Level |
|-------|-----------|-------------|-------------------|---------------|
| **Claude Opus 4.5** | TBD | TBD | ~$10-20 (est.) | Commercial Terms ✅ |
| **Claude Sonnet 4.5** | $3 | $15 | ~$9 | Commercial Terms ✅ |
| **GPT-5.1** | ~$10-15 | ~$30-50 | ~$20-32 | ZDR available ✅ |
| **GPT-5.1-Codex-Max** | TBD | TBD | ~$25-40 (est.) | ZDR available ✅ |
| **Gemini 3.0 Pro** | ~$7 | ~$21 | ~$14 | EU residency ✅ |
| **DeepSeek-V3.2 (API)** | $0.028 | $1.10 | ~$0.56 | ⚠️ China jurisdiction |
| **DeepSeek-V3.2 (Self)** | $0 | $0 | **€500-1000/mo GPU** | ✅ Full control |

---

### **Deployment Cost Estimate (6 Weeks)**

| Task | Claude Opus 4.5 API | GPT-5.1-Codex-Max | Gemini 3.0 Pro (Vertex) | DeepSeek Self-Host |
|------|-------------------|------------------|------------------------|-------------------|
| Architecture (50K tokens) | ~$50 | ~$120 | ~$70 | ~€500/mo |
| Terraform gen (200K tokens) | ~$200 | ~$480 | ~$280 | (same) |
| Ansible gen (150K tokens) | ~$150 | ~$360 | ~$210 | (same) |
| n8n workflows (100K tokens) | ~$100 | ~$240 | ~$140 | (same) |
| Testing (300K tokens) | ~$300 | ~$720 | ~$420 | (same) |
| Docs (50K tokens) | ~$50 | ~$120 | ~$70 | (same) |
| **TOTAL (6 weeks)** | **~$850** | **~$2,040** | **~$1,190** | **~€3,000-6,000** |

**Recommendation:** **Claude Opus 4.5 API** (Commercial Terms) = best privacy/cost/performance balance.

---

## PART 6: FINAL RECOMMENDATIONS

### **For Common Cloud Deployment (Vulnerable Communities)**

**Phase 1 (MVP, Months 1-6):**
- ✅ **Use: Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)**
  - Best coding performance
  - Commercial Terms = no training, minimal retention
  - US jurisdiction (NSL risk) but acceptable for MVP
  - Cost: ~$850 for 6-week deployment

- ⚠️ **Avoid: Consumer interfaces** (ChatGPT.com, Claude.ai, Gemini app)
  - 5-year retention (Claude)
  - Can be subpoenaed (NYT precedent)
  - No privacy guarantees

- ⚠️ **Avoid: DeepSeek API**
  - China jurisdiction
  - Risk to Uyghur, Hong Kong, Taiwan-related orgs

---

**Phase 2 (Scale, Months 6-12):**
- ✅ **Add: Gemini 3.0 Pro (Vertex AI, EU region)**
  - EU data residency
  - GDPR-compliant
  - Better jurisdiction than US-only APIs
  - Cost: ~$1,190 for 6-week deployment

- ✅ **Consider: Self-hosted DeepSeek-V3.2** (if budget allows)
  - Deploy on Iceland/Switzerland servers
  - Zero external data transmission
  - No subpoena risk
  - Cost: €500-1000/mo GPU server (ongoing)

---

**Phase 3 (Mature, Year 2+):**
- ✅ **Migrate to: Self-hosted open-source models**
  - Llama 4 (Meta, open-source)
  - DeepSeek-V3.2 (open-source)
  - Mistral Large 3 (open-source)
  - Deploy on Iceland/Switzerland servers
  - Zero external API calls = maximum privacy

---

## CONCLUSION

**December 2025 Reality:**
- ALL major AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) are US-based, subject to NSLs, FISA courts, and subpoenas[224][225][230][234][239][242]
- Court precedent (NYT v. OpenAI) shows retention promises can be overridden[230][242]
- 5-year retention policy (Claude) creates extended exposure window[225][234][240]
- Consumer interfaces (ChatGPT.com, Claude.ai) have NO privacy guarantees

**Best Practice for Vulnerable Communities:**
1. **Use API with Commercial Terms** (not consumer interfaces)
2. **Rotate API keys monthly** (minimize exposure window)
3. **Log all prompts locally** (your encrypted infrastructure)
4. **Plan migration to self-hosted** (Year 2+, Iceland/Switzerland)
5. **Never use AI for:**
   - Real names of vulnerable individuals
   - Immigration status information
   - Legal strategy discussions (no attorney-client privilege)
   - Medical records (HIPAA violations)
   - Financial data (PCI-DSS violations)

**Nicholas Merrill's Legacy:** "You can't encrypt your way out of bad infrastructure. You have to design privacy into the infrastructure itself."

**For Common Cloud:** Start with Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms) for speed, plan migration to self-hosted DeepSeek-V3.2 on Iceland servers for maximum sovereignty.

---

**End of Document**

*Corrected analysis accounting for actual December 2025 models and privacy policies. Apologies for initial error.*

# AI Model Landscape -- March 6, 2026

## 1. Anthropic (Claude)

**Source:** [Claude Models Overview](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview)

### Current Models

| Model | API Model ID | Released | Context | Max Output | Input $/MTok | Output $/MTok |
|-------|-------------|----------|---------|------------|-------------|--------------|
| **Claude Opus 4.6** | `claude-opus-4-6` | Feb 5, 2026 | 200K (1M beta) | 128K | $5.00 | $25.00 |
| **Claude Sonnet 4.6** | `claude-sonnet-4-6` | Feb 17, 2026 | 200K (1M beta) | 64K | $3.00 | $15.00 |
| **Claude Haiku 4.5** | `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` | Oct 1, 2025 | 200K | 64K | $1.00 | $5.00 |

**Key details:**
- Opus 4.6 is the most intelligent model, best for agents and complex coding. Training data cutoff: Aug 2025, reliable knowledge cutoff: May 2025.
- Sonnet 4.6 is the everyday workhorse -- reportedly outperforms Opus 4.6 on some real-world office tasks. Training data cutoff: Jan 2026, knowledge cutoff: Aug 2025.
- Haiku 4.5 remains from Oct 2025; there is no Haiku 4.6 yet. Knowledge cutoff: Feb 2025.
- All three support extended thinking. Opus and Sonnet 4.6 also support adaptive thinking.

### Notable Legacy Models (still available)
- Claude Sonnet 4.5 (`claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`) -- $3/$15
- Claude Opus 4.5 (`claude-opus-4-5-20251101`) -- $5/$25
- Claude Opus 4.1 (`claude-opus-4-1-20250805`) -- $15/$75
- Claude Sonnet 4 / Opus 4 (May 2025) -- $3/$15 and $15/$75 respectively
- Claude 3 Haiku -- **deprecated**, retiring April 19, 2026

---

## 2. OpenAI

**Sources:** [OpenAI API Pricing](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/pricing) | [GPT-5.4 launch (TechCrunch)](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/05/openai-launches-gpt-5-4-with-pro-and-thinking-versions/) | [GPT-5.2 Codex](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-2-codex/)

### Current Models

| Model | Input $/MTok | Output $/MTok | Notes |
|-------|-------------|--------------|-------|
| **GPT-5.4** | $2.50 | $15.00 | Released **March 5, 2026**. Latest flagship. 1M context. First OpenAI model with native computer-use. |
| **GPT-5.4 Pro** | $30.00 | $180.00 | Premium tier with highest quality |
| **GPT-5.2** | $1.75 | $14.00 | Previous flagship, being replaced by 5.4 |
| **GPT-5.1** | $1.25 | $10.00 | |
| **GPT-5** | $1.25 | $10.00 | Original GPT-5 base |
| **GPT-5-mini** | $0.25 | $2.00 | Budget option |
| **GPT-5-nano** | $0.05 | $0.40 | Ultra-low-cost |
| **GPT-4.1** | $2.00 | $8.00 | |
| **GPT-4.1-mini** | $0.40 | $1.60 | |
| **GPT-4o** | $2.50 | $10.00 | Legacy multimodal |

### Reasoning Models (O-series)

| Model | Input $/MTok | Output $/MTok | Notes |
|-------|-------------|--------------|-------|
| **o3-pro** | $20.00 | $80.00 | Highest reasoning tier |
| **o3** | $2.00 | $8.00 | Strong reasoning model |
| **o4-mini** | $1.10 | $4.40 | Efficient reasoning |
| **o1** | $15.00 | $60.00 | Original reasoning model (legacy) |

**Note:** Reasoning tokens are billed as output tokens but are not visible in API responses.

---

## 3. Google (Gemini)

**Sources:** [Gemini API Pricing](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/pricing) | [Gemini 3.1 Pro info](https://devtk.ai/en/blog/gemini-api-pricing-guide-2026/)

### Current Models

| Model | Model ID | Input $/MTok | Output $/MTok | Notes |
|-------|----------|-------------|--------------|-------|
| **Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview** | `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` | $2.00 (<=200K) / $4.00 | $12.00 / $18.00 | Latest. "Latest performance, intelligence improvements." Feb 2026. |
| **Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview** | `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` | $0.25 | $1.50 | Cost-efficient, high-volume agentic tasks |
| **Gemini 3 Flash Preview** | `gemini-3-flash-preview` | $0.50 | $3.00 | Intelligent + fast. Has free tier. |
| **Gemini 2.5 Pro** | (stable) | $1.25 / $2.50 | $10.00 / $15.00 | Strong reasoning, GA |
| **Gemini 2.5 Flash** | (stable) | $0.30 | $2.50 | Best value in market |
| **Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite** | (stable) | $0.10 | $0.40 | Ultra-budget |

**Key details:**
- Gemini 3 launched January 2026 with major agentic capability improvements.
- Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview arrived February 2026 with 77.1% ARC-AGI-2 score and native video understanding.
- Google offers a genuinely useful free tier through Google AI Studio (unlike Anthropic and OpenAI).
- Consumer plans: Free, Pro ($19.99/mo), Ultra ($124.99/3 months).

---

## 4. DeepSeek

**Sources:** [DeepSeek Models Guide (BentoML)](https://www.bentoml.com/blog/the-complete-guide-to-deepseek-models-from-v3-to-r1-and-beyond) | [DeepSeek V4 status (Evolink)](https://evolink.ai/blog/deepseek-v4-release-window-prep) | [DeepSeek Guide 2026](https://deepseek.ai/blog/deepseek-guide-2026)

### Current Models

| Model | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| **DeepSeek V3.1** | Released Aug 21, 2025 | MIT License. Hybrid thinking/non-thinking modes. Surpasses V3 and R1 by 40%+ on SWE-bench. |
| **DeepSeek V3** (V3-0324) | Available | 671B MoE. Incorporates R1 RL techniques. Strong general-purpose + coding. |
| **DeepSeek R1** | Available | Dedicated reasoning model. OpenAI o1-level performance. |
| **DeepSeek VL2** | Available | Multimodal (vision-language) understanding. |
| **DeepSeek V4** | **NOT YET RELEASED** | Originally targeted mid-Feb 2026, delayed. Expected ~1 trillion params, 1M context, hybrid reasoning. Community consensus: early March 2026 but unconfirmed. |
| **DeepSeek R2** | **NOT YET RELEASED** | Delayed due to data labeling and chip constraints. |

**Pricing:** DeepSeek remains dramatically cheaper than Western competitors. Current V3 API pricing is approximately $0.55/MTok input (vs. $3+ for comparable Western models). V4 pricing not yet announced but expected within 30% of V3 rates.

---

## 5. Meta (Llama 4)

**Sources:** [Meta Llama 4 announcement](https://ai.meta.com/blog/llama-4-multimodal-intelligence/) | [Llama 4 models page](https://www.llama.com/models/llama-4/) | [LlamaCon announcements](https://ai.meta.com/blog/llamacon-llama-news/)

### Current Models (Spring 2025 release, open-weight)

| Model | Active Params | Experts | Notes |
|-------|--------------|---------|-------|
| **Llama 4 Scout** | 17B | 16 (MoE) | Best-in-class multimodal for its size. 10M context window. Fits on single H100. Beats Gemma 3, Gemini 2.0 Flash-Lite, Mistral 3.1. |
| **Llama 4 Maverick** | 17B | 128 (MoE) | Best multimodal in its class. Beats GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Flash on reported benchmarks. |
| **Llama 4 Behemoth** | 288B | 16 (MoE) | Most powerful. Outperforms GPT-4.5, Claude Sonnet 3.7, Gemini 2.0 Pro on STEM benchmarks. |

**Key details:**
- All Llama 4 models are natively multimodal (not bolted-on vision).
- Scout and Maverick available on Hugging Face for download.
- Free and open-weight. No API pricing (self-hosted or via third-party providers like Groq, Cerebras, Together).
- Llama 4 uses Mixture-of-Experts architecture, a departure from Llama 3's dense architecture.

---

## 6. Mistral AI

**Sources:** [Mistral 3 announcement](https://mistral.ai/news/mistral-3) | [Mistral models page](https://mistral.ai/models) | [TechCrunch coverage](https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/02/mistral-closes-in-on-big-ai-rivals-with-mistral-3-open-weight-frontier-and-small-models/)

### Current Models (December 2025 release)

| Model | Parameters | Notes |
|-------|-----------|-------|
| **Mistral Large 3** | 41B active / 675B total (MoE) | Flagship. 256K context. #2 in OSS non-reasoning models on LMArena. Apache 2.0. |
| **Ministral 3 14B** | 14B (dense) | Base, instruct, and reasoning variants. Multimodal + multilingual. |
| **Ministral 3 8B** | 8B (dense) | Same variant options as 14B. |
| **Ministral 3 3B** | 3B (dense) | Edge-friendly. Same variant options. |
| **Devstral 2** | (not specified) | Coding-focused. Dec 10, 2025. |
| **Devstral Small 2** | 24B | Coding model. Claims better performance than Qwen 3 Coder Flash. |

**Key details:**
- All Mistral 3 models are Apache 2.0 licensed (fully open-source).
- Each Ministral 3 size comes in base, instruct, and specialized reasoning variants.
- Native multimodal and multilingual capabilities across the family.

---

## Summary of Confidence Levels

| Claim | Confidence |
|-------|-----------|
| Claude Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6 details and pricing | **Verified** (official docs) |
| Claude Haiku 4.5 is the current Haiku (no 4.6) | **Verified** (official docs) |
| OpenAI GPT-5.4 released March 5, 2026 | **Verified** (multiple major outlets) |
| OpenAI pricing table | **Verified** (official pricing page) |
| Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview exists with pricing | **Verified** (official pricing page) |
| DeepSeek V3.1 released Aug 2025 | **Verified** (multiple sources) |
| DeepSeek V4 not yet released | **High confidence** -- all sources say delayed past Feb target, no launch confirmed |
| DeepSeek R2 not yet released | **High confidence** -- no release announcements found |
| Llama 4 Scout/Maverick/Behemoth details | **Verified** (official Meta blog) |
| Mistral 3 family details | **Verified** (official Mistral announcement) |

**Notable uncertainty:** DeepSeek V4's exact release timing remains unclear. It was expected in February 2026 but has been delayed. Some community sources suggest early March 2026 but nothing is confirmed by DeepSeek.

# AI Provider Privacy Policies & Data Retention Practices -- March 2026

## 1. Anthropic / Claude

**Policy effective date:** January 12, 2026 ([Updates to our Privacy Policy](https://privacy.claude.com/en/articles/10301952-updates-to-our-privacy-policy))

### Data Retention Periods

| Scenario | Retention Period |
|----------|-----------------|
| Deleted conversations (consumer) | Removed from chat history immediately; backend deletion within **30 days** |
| Training opt-in (de-identified data) | Up to **5 years** in model training pipelines |
| Usage policy violations (inputs/outputs) | Up to **2 years** |
| Trust and safety scores | Up to **7 years** |
| User feedback submissions | **5 years** |

Source: [How long do you store my data?](https://privacy.claude.com/en/articles/10023548-how-long-do-you-store-my-data)

### 5-Year Retention -- Still In Effect, But Conditional

Yes, the 5-year retention is still in effect for consumer accounts, but **only applies if the user opts in to model training**. The September 28, 2025 update introduced the opt-in toggle ("You can help improve Claude"). If you decline, the standard 30-day deletion applies. The 5-year retention only covers **new or resumed chats** after the setting is enabled, not retroactive conversations.

### Commercial / Enterprise Terms

Anthropic's consumer privacy policy **does not apply** to commercial customers (Claude for Work, Claude for Education, Claude Gov, API via Amazon Bedrock or Google Vertex AI). For these, Anthropic acts as a **data processor**, governed by separate Data Processing Agreements. Enterprise data is **not used for training** under these agreements.

Sources: [Anthropic Privacy Policy](https://www.anthropic.com/legal/privacy), [Updates to Consumer Terms](https://www.anthropic.com/news/updates-to-our-consumer-terms)

### Changes Since September 2025

- **Sep 28, 2025**: Major update -- introduced opt-in model training for consumers, 5-year de-identified data retention, renamed "Notice on Model Training" to "Non-User Privacy Policy"
- **Oct 8, 2025**: Minor clarifications to Section 10 (user communications)
- **Jan 12, 2026**: Added Consumer Health Data Privacy Policy for US users integrating third-party health apps; consolidated regional supplemental disclosures

### Training Opt-Out

Available via account settings. **Exception**: Even opted-out conversations are used for training if (1) flagged for safety review or (2) explicitly reported by the user.

### Jurisdiction

- **EEA/UK/Switzerland**: Anthropic Ireland, Limited
- **All other regions**: Anthropic PBC (San Francisco, CA)
- Cross-border transfers via adequacy decisions and Standard Contractual Clauses

---

## 2. OpenAI / ChatGPT

**DPA effective date:** January 1, 2026 ([OpenAI Data Processing Addendum](https://openai.com/policies/data-processing-addendum/))

### Data Retention Periods

| Scenario | Retention Period |
|----------|-----------------|
| API standard | **30 days** for safety monitoring, then auto-deleted |
| ChatGPT consumer (default) | Retained indefinitely for model improvement (unless chat history is turned off) |
| ChatGPT with history off | Retained **30 days** for abuse monitoring, then deleted |
| Zero Data Retention (API/Enterprise) | **Not retained at all** -- no storage of prompts or responses |

Sources: [OpenAI Privacy Policy](https://openai.com/policies/row-privacy-policy/), [Data controls in the OpenAI platform](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/your-data/)

### Zero Data Retention Status

ZDR remains available for qualifying enterprise and API customers. Key details:
- Data processed under ZDR is **not stored** beyond immediate processing
- **No human review** of ZDR-processed data
- Requires **prior approval** by OpenAI and acceptance of additional requirements
- Not automatically applied; must be requested through sales
- Available for **eligible endpoints** only (not all API endpoints qualify)

Source: [Enterprise privacy at OpenAI](https://openai.com/enterprise-privacy/)

### Training Opt-Out

- **API customers**: Data is **not used for training by default** (since March 2023)
- **ChatGPT consumer**: Users can disable chat history in settings; conversations with history off are not used for training (retained 30 days for abuse monitoring only)
- **Enterprise/Team**: Data is never used for training

### Notable Legal Development

A US court order (stemming from the NYT copyright lawsuit) required OpenAI to **preserve all user interactions** including deleted conversations. Only Enterprise, Education, and ZDR customers are excluded from this preservation order. OpenAI appealed in mid-2025. This effectively means consumer ChatGPT data may be retained longer than stated policy due to litigation hold.

Sources: [OpenAI response to NYT data demands](https://openai.com/index/response-to-nyt-data-demands/), [OpenAI Government User Data Request Policy](https://cdn.openai.com/pdf/openai-law-enforcement-policy-v.2025-12.pdf)

### Jurisdiction

- US-based (San Francisco, CA)
- GDPR-specific provisions via separate ROW privacy policy
- Data Processing Addendum available for enterprise customers

---

## 3. Google / Gemini

### Consumer Gemini Data Retention

| Scenario | Retention Period |
|----------|-----------------|
| Gemini consumer (default) | Prompts and responses stored up to **30 days** for debugging and abuse detection |
| Workspace Enterprise | Admins can **shorten or disable** prompt storage entirely |
| Vertex AI (in-memory cache) | **24-hour TTL**, in-memory only (not at-rest), project-level isolation |
| Vertex AI Zero Data Retention | **No retention** -- must be specified per API call |

### Training Policy

Google states it will **not use customer data to train or fine-tune AI/ML models** without prior permission or instruction, as outlined in the Service Terms. This is a blanket commitment covering both Workspace and Cloud customers.

Source: [Google Cloud GenAI Privacy Whitepaper](https://services.google.com/fh/files/misc/genai_privacy_google_cloud.pdf)

### Vertex AI Privacy

- Zero Data Retention must be explicitly enabled **per API call** by setting a specific field in the request
- Session Resumption must **not** be enabled to achieve true ZDR
- Data cached in-memory only, isolated per project, 24-hour TTL
- SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA available

Source: [Vertex AI Zero Data Retention](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/vertex-ai-zero-data-retention)

### EU Data Residency

As of June 2025, Google supports data regionalization for Gemini features in Workspace:
- Configurable data regions: **US**, **EU**, or **No preference**
- Vertex AI Platform supports configurable data location for AI services
- Admins configure data regions at the organizational level

Source: [Data residency for Vertex AI](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/learn/data-residency), [Gemini in Workspace Privacy Hub](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en)

### Jurisdiction

- Google LLC / Google Ireland Limited (for EEA users)
- Comprehensive GDPR compliance with DPAs, SCCs, and adequacy decisions

---

## 4. DeepSeek

**Privacy Policy:** [DeepSeek Privacy Policy](https://cdn.deepseek.com/policies/en-US/deepseek-privacy-policy.html)

### Data Retention

DeepSeek provides **no specific retention periods**. The policy states data is retained "as long as necessary to provide our Services" and for legal obligations, legitimate business interests, and legal claims. When no longer needed, data is "destroyed, deleted, or anonymized." This vagueness has drawn significant regulatory criticism.

### Data Storage

All personal data is **stored in the People's Republic of China**. The policy explicitly states: "We directly collect, process and store your Personal Data in People's Republic of China."

### Data Collected

- Account info (birth date, username, email, phone, password)
- User inputs (text, voice, prompts, uploaded files, chat history)
- Device/network info (IP, device model, OS, device ID)
- Approximate location via IP
- Cookies and tracking technologies
- Payment transaction data

### Training Data Usage

DeepSeek uses personal data "to improve and develop the Services and to train and improve our technology, such as our machine learning models and algorithms." Users in applicable jurisdictions have a right to opt out, but the mechanism and effectiveness are unclear.

### Regulatory Actions (Major)

| Country/Region | Action | Status |
|----------------|--------|--------|
| **Italy** | Garante blocked DeepSeek R1 from app stores (Jan 2025) for GDPR non-compliance | **Banned** -- app removed |
| **South Korea** | PIPC ordered removal from Apple/Google app stores (Feb 2025); defense ministry banned on military computers | **Banned** -- under investigation |
| **Belgium, France, Ireland** | Regulatory inquiries into data processing practices | **Under investigation** |
| **US** | No federal ban, but multiple agencies expressing concerns about national security and data sovereignty | **Under scrutiny** |

Sources: [EU Regulators Scrutinize DeepSeek](https://usercentrics.com/knowledge-hub/eu-regulators-scrutinize-deepseek-for-data-privacy-violations/), [DeepSeek Banned Countries 2026](https://www.demandsage.com/deepseek-banned-countries/), [Italy and South Korea ban DeepSeek](https://www.privacylaws.com/news/italy-and-south-korea-ban-deepseek-and-start-investigation/)

DeepSeek has claimed GDPR does not apply to it since it is based in China, but EU regulators have countered that GDPR's extraterritorial scope applies because DeepSeek processes EU residents' data. DeepSeek has appointed Prighter Group as its GDPR Article 27 representative (rep_deepseek@prighter.com).

### Jurisdiction

Hangzhou DeepSeek Artificial Intelligence Co., Ltd. (China). Subject to Chinese national security laws that could compel data disclosure to the government.

---

## Comparative Summary

| Factor | Anthropic | OpenAI | Google/Gemini | DeepSeek |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|----------|
| **Default consumer retention** | 30 days (5yr if training opt-in) | Indefinite (30 days if history off) | 30 days | Unspecified ("as long as necessary") |
| **Enterprise/API retention** | Per DPA (processor) | 30 days API / ZDR available | 24hr cache / ZDR available | N/A |
| **Training opt-out** | Yes (account toggle) | Yes (history toggle / API default off) | Yes (not used without permission) | Claimed, unclear mechanism |
| **ZDR option** | N/A for consumer | Yes (API/Enterprise, by approval) | Yes (per-call, Vertex AI) | No |
| **Data stored in** | US (SF) / Ireland (EEA) | US | US/EU (configurable) | **China** |
| **GDPR entity** | Anthropic Ireland Ltd | OpenAI Ireland Ltd | Google Ireland Ltd | Art. 27 rep only (Prighter) |
| **EU data residency** | Via SCCs | Via SCCs/DPA | Native EU region support | No |
| **Notable risk** | 5yr retention if opted in | Litigation hold (NYT case) | Complex per-call ZDR config | Chinese jurisdiction, bans in IT/KR |
| **Policy last updated** | Jan 12, 2026 | Jan 1, 2026 (DPA) | Ongoing (Workspace Jun 2025) | Not clearly dated |

### Key Changes Since December 2025

1. **Anthropic** (Jan 2026): Added Consumer Health Data Privacy Policy for US users; consolidated regional disclosures. The major structural change (opt-in training, 5yr retention) happened in Sep 2025.
2. **OpenAI** (Jan 2026): Updated Data Processing Addendum; new Government User Data Request Policy effective Jan 1, 2026. Ongoing NYT litigation hold remains a wildcard for consumer data.
3. **Google**: No major Gemini privacy policy changes since EU data residency rollout in Jun 2025. Vertex AI ZDR documentation refined.
4. **DeepSeek**: Regulatory pressure continues to escalate. As of early 2026, still banned in Italy and South Korea, under investigation in multiple EU states. No substantive policy improvements observed.
