DeepSeek has appointed Prighter Group as its GDPR Article 27 representative (rep_deepseek@prighter.com).

### Jurisdiction

Hangzhou DeepSeek Artificial Intelligence Co., Ltd. (China). Subject to Chinese national security laws that could compel data disclosure to the government.

---

## Comparative Summary

| Factor | Anthropic | OpenAI | Google/Gemini | DeepSeek |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|----------|
| **Default consumer retention** | 30 days (5yr if training opt-in) | Indefinite (30 days if history off) | 30 days | Unspecified ("as long as necessary") |
| **Enterprise/API retention** | Per DPA (processor) | 30 days API / ZDR available | 24hr cache / ZDR available | N/A |
| **Training opt-out** | Yes (account toggle) | Yes (history toggle / API default off) | Yes (not used without permission) | Claimed, unclear mechanism |
| **ZDR option** | N/A for consumer | Yes (API/Enterprise, by approval) | Yes (per-call, Vertex AI) | No |
| **Data stored in** | US (SF) / Ireland (EEA) | US | US/EU (configurable) | **China** |
| **GDPR entity** | Anthropic Ireland Ltd | OpenAI Ireland Ltd | Google Ireland Ltd | Art. 27 rep only (Prighter) |
| **EU data residency** | Via SCCs | Via SCCs/DPA | Native EU region support | No |
| **Notable risk** | 5yr retention if opted in | Litigation hold (NYT case) | Complex per-call ZDR config | Chinese jurisdiction, bans in IT/KR |
| **Policy last updated** | Jan 12, 2026 | Jan 1, 2026 (DPA) | Ongoing (Workspace Jun 2025) | Not clearly dated |

### Key Changes Since December 2025

1. **Anthropic** (Jan 2026): Added Consumer Health Data Privacy Policy for US users; consolidated regional disclosures. The major structural change (opt-in training, 5yr retention) happened in Sep 2025.
2. **OpenAI** (Jan 2026): Updated Data Processing Addendum; new Government User Data Request Policy effective Jan 1, 2026. Ongoing NYT litigation hold remains a wildcard for consumer data.
3. **Google**: No major Gemini privacy policy changes since EU data residency rollout in Jun 2025. Vertex AI ZDR documentation refined.
4. **DeepSeek**: Regulatory pressure continues to escalate. As of early 2026, still banned in Italy and South Korea, under investigation in multiple EU states. No substantive policy improvements observed.

Now I have comprehensive coverage. Here is the full report:

---

# Comprehensive Report: AI Companies, Privacy, and the Courts (as of March 2026)

## 1. NYT v. OpenAI (S.D.N.Y.)

**Status: Active -- Discovery phase, no trial date set.**

- **March 2025:** Judge allowed the copyright case to go forward, denying OpenAI's motion to dismiss. ([NPR](https://www.npr.org/2025/03/26/nx-s1-5288157/new-york-times-openai-copyright-case-goes-forward))
- **January 2026:** Judge Sidney Stein upheld a magistrate's order compelling OpenAI to produce its **entire 20-million-log sample** of ChatGPT conversations, rejecting OpenAI's attempt to hand over only cherry-picked logs. This was a major discovery victory for the plaintiffs. ([National Law Review](https://natlawreview.com/article/openai-loses-privacy-gambit-20-million-chatgpt-logs-likely-headed-copyright), [Law.com](https://www.law.com/legaltechnews/2026/01/07/openai-must-turn-over-millions-of-chat-logs-in-copyright-litigation-ny-federal-judge-rules-/?slreturn=20260110090802), [Lawyer Monthly](https://www.lawyer-monthly.com/2026/01/openai-sdny-discovery-20m-chat-logs-legal-impact/))
- **March 2026:** OpenAI is now seeking to depose the NYT's expert consultant and demanding the specific ChatGPT prompts/outputs used to create the complaint's exhibit showing verbatim "regurgitation" of Times articles. ([National Today](https://nationaltoday.com/us/ny/new-york/news/2026/03/04/openai-seeks-access-to-nyts-chatgpt-prompts-in-copyright-lawsuit/))
- **No trial date set.** No final ruling on fair use has been issued. ([Medium - Patent AI Lab](https://medium.com/@patentailab/nyt-vs-openai-lawsuit-update-2026-did-regurgitation-kill-the-fair-use-defense-d63ff021b805))

---

## 2. Major AI Copyright / Training Data Cases

### Bartz v. Anthropic -- $1.5 Billion Settlement (N.D. Cal.)
- **June 2025:** Judge William Alsup ruled that using copyrighted books to train LLMs is **"transformative -- spectacularly so"** and constitutes fair use **if the works were legally acquired**. However, he denied Anthropic's summary judgment on the piracy angle, finding that downloading millions of pirated books from shadow libraries was **not** fair use. ([IPWatchdog](https://ipwatchdog.com/2025/12/23/copyright-ai-collide-three-key-decisions-ai-training-copyrighted-content-2025/))
- **August 2025:** Anthropic agreed to a historic **$1.5 billion settlement** -- roughly $3,000 per title for ~500,000 books. ([Copyright Alliance](https://copyrightalliance.org/participating-bartz-v-anthropic-settlement/), [Authors Guild](https://authorsguild.org/advocacy/artificial-intelligence/what-authors-need-to-know-about-the-anthropic-settlement/), [NPR](https://www.npr.org/2025/09/05/nx-s1-5529404/anthropic-settlement-authors-copyright-ai))
- **March 2026:** Claims deadline is March 30, 2026. Fairness hearing set for April 2026. Preliminary approval granted; final approval pending. ([Anthropic Settlement Site](https://www.anthropiccopyrightsettlement.com/))

### Kadrey v. Meta (N.D. Cal.)
- **June 2025:** Judge Chhabria found Meta's use of copyrighted works to train its LLM was **highly transformative and fair use**, though he noted transformation alone is not dispositive. ([Skadden](https://www.skadden.com/insights/publications/2025/07/fair-use-and-ai-training))

### Thomson Reuters v. ROSS Intelligence (D. Del. / 3rd Circuit)
- Trial court held training use was **not fair use**. Currently **on appeal** in the Third Circuit, where questions of copyrightability and fair use will be decided. ([Copyright Alliance](https://copyrightalliance.org/ai-copyright-lawsuit-developments-2025/))

### Andersen v. Stability AI, Midjourney, DeviantArt (N.D. Cal.)
- **August 2024:** Judge refused to dismiss core copyright claims, allowing discovery to proceed.
- **August 2025:** Midjourney filed a 43-page answer leaning on fair use and the First Amendment (no motion to dismiss).
- **Late 2025:** Parties in active document discovery. ([McKool Smith](https://www.mckoolsmith.com/newsroom-ailitigation-46), [NYU JIPEL](https://jipel.law.nyu.edu/andersen-v-stability-ai-the-landmark-case-unpacking-the-copyright-risks-of-ai-image-generators/))

### Disney/Universal/Warner Bros. v. Midjourney
- **June 2025:** Disney and Universal filed a complaint alleging Midjourney reproduces Marvel and Star Wars characters.
- **November 2025:** Warner Bros. case consolidated with Disney's. ([McKool Smith](https://www.mckoolsmith.com/newsroom-ailitigation-46))

### Reddit v. Anthropic (California state court)
- Reddit sued Anthropic for breach of contract, unjust enrichment, trespass to chattels, and unfair competition, alleging Anthropic deployed automated bots to scrape Reddit content over 100,000 times despite publicly claiming it does not scrape Reddit. ([Fortune](https://fortune.com/2026/02/24/anthropic-china-deepseek-theft-claude-distillation-copyright-national-security/))

### Music Publishers v. Anthropic (M.D. Tenn.)
- UMG, Concord, and others sued Anthropic in October 2023 for massive-scale music lyric copyright infringement via web scraping. Case ongoing. ([Copyright Alliance](https://copyrightalliance.org/ai-copyright-lawsuit-developments-2025/))

**Overall landscape:** As of October 2025, there were at least **51 copyright lawsuits** against AI companies. No court is expected to issue a final fair use ruling in AI training until **summer 2026 at the earliest**. ([Chat GPT Is Eating the World](https://chatgptiseatingtheworld.com/2025/10/08/status-of-all-51-copyright-lawsuits-v-ai-oct-8-2025-no-more-decisions-on-fair-use-in-2025/))

---

## 3. AI Chat History as Evidence -- New Precedents

### US v. Heppner (S.D.N.Y.) -- Landmark Privilege Ruling
- **February 10, 2026:** Judge Jed Rakoff ruled that **conversations between a criminal defendant and Anthropic's Claude are not protected** by attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine. Reasoning: Claude is not a lawyer, and "discussion of legal issues between two non-attorneys is not protected." The defendant had no reasonable expectation of confidentiality. ([HSF Kramer](https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/litigation/2026-02/new-york-court-finds-client-chats-with-generative-ai-tool-claude-are-not-privileged), [Crowell & Moring](https://www.crowell.com/en/insights/client-alerts/federal-court-rules-some-ai-chats-are-not-protected-by-legal-privilege-what-it-means-for-you))

### Criminal Cases
- From January to June 2025, OpenAI received **119 requests for user account information, 26 requests for chat content, and 1 emergency request** from law enforcement.
- Three 2025 criminal prosecutions cited ChatGPT conversations as evidence in cases involving **child exploitation, arson, and vandalism**. ([Kolmogorov Law](https://www.kolmogorovlaw.com/the-chatgpt-subpoena-revolution-when-your-ai-conversations-become-court-evidence), [Van Ho Law](https://www.vanholaw.com/blog/2026/february/when-chatbots-become-witnesses-how-ai-conversati/))

### FBI Seizure of CEO's AI Chats
- The FBI seized a CEO's computer and sought access to his AI chat history. Defense attorneys argued attorney-client privilege; the court left only a **narrow, untested opening** -- if an attorney specifically directed the client to conduct AI research as part of attorney work product. ([California Employment Law Report](https://www.californiaemploymentlawreport.com/2026/02/the-fbi-seized-a-ceos-ai-chats-and-four-more-reasons-california-employers-cant-ignore-ai-any-longer/))

### Divorce / Family Law
- California courts are grappling with AI-generated evidence in family law. By **July 2025**, all California courts serving populations over 500,000 must implement basic AI document review capabilities, with full statewide implementation by December 2026. ([Lass Law](https://www.lasslaw.com/ai-in-the-courtroom-how-californias-new-2025-rule-may-change-your-divorce-strategy/), [Dentons](https://www.dentons.com/en/insights/newsletters/2026/february/23/divorce-and-family-law-insights/what-happens-when-you-ask-ai-about-your-divorce))

### Immigration
- USCIS deployed an **Evidence Classifier** AI system that doubled the rate of 30-day application processing (30% to 58%) and saved 13,000+ hours. AI outputs (including chat messages, emails) are subject to mandatory human review before enforcement action. ([EB-5 Insights](https://www.eb5insights.com/2025/12/18/the-us-governments-expanding-use-of-ai-in-immigration-adjudications-considerations-for-employers-and-investors/))

### Key Takeaway
Sam Altman publicly warned in a 2025 podcast that people treating ChatGPT like a therapist or lawyer should understand those conversations **can be subpoenaed**. A survey found **half of AI users are unaware** their chats can be compelled as evidence. ([Bressler](https://www.bressler.com/publication-the-new-frontier-of-evidence-ai-chat-histories))

---

## 4. Regulatory Actions

### FTC Actions Against AI Companies
- **September 2025:** FTC launched a formal inquiry into **AI companion chatbots**, ordering 7 companies to provide information on impacts to children and youth. ([FTC](https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2025/09/ftc-launches-inquiry-ai-chatbots-acting-companions))
- **August 2025:** FTC approved final orders against **Workado LLC** (false "98% accuracy" claims for AI content detection -- actual accuracy ~53%) and filed suit against **Air AI Technologies**. ([Perkins Coie](https://perkinscoie.com/insights/blog/privacy-law-recap-2025-ftc-enforcement))
- **December 2025:** FTC reopened and set aside a 2024 consent order involving **Rytr LLC**, signaling a shift in enforcement approach. ([Fox Rothschild](https://dataprivacy.foxrothschild.com/2026/01/articles/general-privacy-data-security-news-developments/ftc-dismissal-of-settlement-with-ai-company-signals-shift-in-enforcement-focus/))

### Character.AI Teen Suicide Lawsuits -- Settlement
- Multiple lawsuits filed after **teen suicides** linked to Character.AI chatbots (Sewell Setzer III, age 14, Florida; Juliana Peralta, age 13, Colorado; a 17-year-old autistic teen in Texas).
- **January 2026:** Character.AI and Google agreed to **settle** the lawsuits. ([CNN](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/07/business/character-ai-google-settle-teen-suicide-lawsuit), [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/07/google-characterai-to-settle-suits-involving-suicides-ai-chatbots.html))
- Character.AI banned under-18 open-ended chats in late 2025, criticized as too late. ([TorHoerman Law](https://www.torhoermanlaw.com/ai-lawsuit/character-ai-lawsuit/))

### EU AI Act Enforcement
- **February 2, 2025:** Prohibited practices (Article 5) became enforceable.
- **August 2, 2025:** Full enforcement of prohibited practices began.
- **August 2, 2026:** High-risk AI system requirements (Annex III) become enforceable -- employment, credit, education, law enforcement AI.
- **No enforcement actions announced yet**, but investigations are reportedly underway into workplace emotion recognition systems, predictive policing algorithms, and social scoring in employee management. ([Secure Privacy](https://secureprivacy.ai/blog/eu-ai-act-2026-compliance), [Legal Nodes](https://www.legalnodes.com/article/eu-ai-act-2026-updates-compliance-requirements-and-business-risks))
- **Penalties:** Up to 35 million EUR or 7% of global turnover for prohibited AI violations.

### GDPR Enforcement Against AI Providers

**Italian DPA (Garante):**
- **December 2024:** Fined OpenAI **15 million EUR** for GDPR violations (no legal basis for training on personal data, failure to notify of March 2023 breach, no age verification). OpenAI is appealing. ([Euronews](https://www.euronews.com/next/2024/12/20/italys-privacy-watchdog-fines-openai-15-million-after-probe-into-chatgpt-data-collection))
- **April/May 2025:** Fined **Replika (Luka Inc.) 5 million EUR** for GDPR violations -- no valid legal basis for data processing, transparency failures, and failure to implement age verification despite risks to minors. ([EDPB](https://www.edpb.europa.eu/news/national-news/2025/ai-italian-supervisory-authority-fines-company-behind-chatbot-replika_en))
- **January 30, 2025:** **Banned DeepSeek** in Italy, imposing a definitive limitation on processing Italian users' personal data. DeepSeek claimed it didn't operate in Italy; Garante disagreed. DeepSeek's privacy policy disclosed data storage in China. ([Bird & Bird](https://www.twobirds.com/en/insights/2025/the-garante-imposes-a-definitive-limitation-on-the-processing-of-italian-users%E2%80%99-personal-data))

**Other EU DPAs on DeepSeek:**
- **Ireland:** DPC sent information request to DeepSeek (Jan 29, 2025).
- **Portugal:** DECO Proteste filed complaint (Jan 31, 2025).
- **Spain:** OCU filed complaint with AEPD (Feb 3, 2025).
- **Germany (Berlin):** Asked DeepSeek to voluntarily remove apps (May 6, 2025); sent notice-and-action to Apple/Google (June 27, 2025). ([Usercentrics](https://usercentrics.com/knowledge-hub/eu-regulators-scrutinize-deepseek-for-data-privacy-violations/), [MIAI](https://ai-regulation.com/deepseek-one-year-later-regulatory-storm-global-surge/))

### DeepSeek Bans (United States)
- **Federal:** "No DeepSeek on Government Devices Act" (HR 1121) introduced February 7, 2025 by Reps. Gottheimer and LaHood. Navy and Commerce Department banned it on government devices. 21 state AGs urged Congress to pass the bill. ([Gottheimer.house.gov](https://gottheimer.house.gov/posts/release-gottheimer-lahood-introduce-new-bipartisan-legislation-to-protect-americans-from-deepseek), [NBC News](https://www.nbcnews.com/business/business-news/us-lawmakers-move-ban-deepseek-government-devices-chinese-surveillance-rcna190965))
- **State-level:** Texas became the first state to ban DeepSeek on all state devices (Jan 31, 2025). New York, Tennessee, Virginia, and others followed. ([StateTech](https://statetechmagazine.com/article/2025/04/these-states-have-banned-deepseek), [National Law Review](https://natlawreview.com/article/three-states-ban-deepseek-use-state-devices-and-networks))
- **Security finding:** Third-party analysis found DeepSeek's app can capture login information and share it with China Mobile (state-owned). ([Conference Board](https://www.conference-board.org/research/CED-Newsletters-Alerts/state-and-federal-governments-deepseak-ban))

### State-Level AI Privacy Laws
- **California:** SB 53 (Transparency in Frontier AI Act) signed September 2025, effective January 1, 2026 -- requires disclosure of risk management protocols for frontier models (revenue >$500M). AB 2013 (GAI Training Data Transparency Act) also effective Jan 1, 2026. AI Transparency Act (SB 942) delayed to August 2, 2026. ([National Law Review](https://natlawreview.com/article/new-california-ai-laws-taking-effect-2026))
- **Texas:** RAIGA (Responsible AI Governance Act) effective January 1, 2026 -- prohibits AI systems that incite harm, infringe constitutional rights, produce deepfakes or CSAM. ([Miller Nash](https://www.millernash.com/industry-news/from-colorado-to-texas-how-states-are-rewriting-ai-laws))
- **Colorado:** Comprehensive AI Act coming online June 2026.
- **Illinois:** Employer AI disclosure requirements starting January 2026.
- **Federal preemption uncertainty:** Trump's December 11, 2025 executive order seeks to preempt state AI laws; enforceability TBD by courts. ([King & Spalding](https://www.kslaw.com/news-and-insights/new-state-ai-laws-are-effective-on-january-1-2026-but-a-new-executive-order-signals-disruption))

---

## 5. National Security Letters / FISA / Surveillance

### NSLs Served to AI Companies
- **No public reporting** has confirmed NSLs specifically served to AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). These letters come with gag orders, so absence of public information is expected. No AI company has published a warrant canary statement confirming or denying receipt of NSLs as of this writing.

### OpenAI Transparency Report (H1 2025)
- OpenAI published its government requests report for January-June 2025. Policy: requires subpoena for account info, valid warrant for content. 119 account info requests, 26 content requests, 1 emergency request received. ([OpenAI](https://cdn.openai.com/trust-and-transparency/report-2025h1-government-requests-for-user-data.pdf))
- OpenAI also operates a **monitoring system that scans all ChatGPT conversations** for potentially harmful content, escalating concerning conversations to human reviewers who can report to law enforcement. ([EFF](https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2025/12/ai-chatbot-companies-should-protect-your-conversations-bulk-surveillance))

### Anthropic Transparency
- Anthropic published a government requests report (latest publicly available covers Jan-June 2024). Anthropic processes requests in accordance with applicable law and may disclose in emergencies involving danger of death or serious physical injury. ([Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/transparency/platform-security))

### OpenAI-Pentagon Deal (February-March 2026)
- After the Trump administration **blacklisted Anthropic** from a Pentagon contract because Anthropic refused to allow its AI to be used for autonomous weapons or mass domestic surveillance, OpenAI announced a deal to provide AI for classified Pentagon networks.
- OpenAI stated three "red lines": no mass domestic surveillance, no autonomous weapons direction. But the EFF criticized the contract language as "weasel words" -- it does not give OpenAI a free-standing right to prohibit otherwise-lawful government use, only stating the Pentagon cannot use OpenAI tech to break existing laws. ([MIT Technology Review](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/03/02/1133850/openais-compromise-with-the-pentagon-is-what-anthropic-feared/), [EFF](https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2026/03/weasel-words-openais-pentagon-deal-wont-stop-ai-powered-surveillance), [NPR](https://www.npr.org/2026/02/27/nx-s1-5729118/trump-anthropic-pentagon-openai-ai-weapons-ban))
- Altman admitted the deal "looked opportunistic and sloppy." ([CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/03/openai-sam-altman-pentagon-deal-amended-surveillance-limits.html))
- **FISA Section 702** is due to expire April 2026 if not renewed, which will generate significant debate. ([Perkins Coie](https://perkinscoie.com/insights/blog/privacy-and-data-security-recap-2025-national-security))

---

## 6. Additional Notable Cases

### Anthropic v. DeepSeek, Moonshot, MiniMax (Distillation Accusations)
- **February 2026:** Anthropic publicly accused three Chinese AI labs of **industrial-scale distillation attacks** -- using ~24,000 fraudulent accounts to generate 16 million+ exchanges with Claude to extract capabilities for their own models. DeepSeek specifically used Claude to generate censorship-safe alternatives for politically sensitive queries. Not a lawsuit (yet) but a public accusation with potential legal ramifications. ([CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/24/anthropic-openai-china-firms-distillation-deepseek.html), [Fortune](https://fortune.com/2026/02/24/anthropic-china-deepseek-theft-claude-distillation-copyright-national-security/), [The Hacker News](https://thehackernews.com/2026/02/anthropic-says-chinese-ai-firms-used-16.html))

### Google Gemini Privacy Lawsuit (N.D. Cal.)
- **October 2025:** Class action filed alleging Google **activated Gemini AI features** across Gmail, Chat, and Meet without user consent, enabling AI to read private communications. Putative class of all U.S. Google account holders. ([National Law Review](https://natlawreview.com/article/silent-switch-new-lawsuit-alleges-google-uses-gemini-ai-secretly-read-gmail-chat))

### Google $425.7M Data Privacy Verdict
- **December 2025:** Federal jury awarded **$425.7 million** to ~98 million Google users who were misled about data collection opt-outs on mobile devices. ([For The People](https://www.forthepeople.com/blog/google-users-win-4257m-verdict-data-privacy-lawsuit/))

### Meta BIPA Litigation
- **2025-2026:** Multiple ongoing BIPA class actions against Meta for biometric data collection (voice, facial recognition) without consent. Illinois courts rejected Meta's California choice-of-law defense. Prior settlements total $700M+. ([DLA Piper](https://privacymatters.dlapiper.com/2026/02/illinois-law-trumps-metas-california-choice-of-law-provision-in-bipa-class-action/))
- **2025 BIPA landscape:** 107+ new BIPA class actions filed in Illinois in 2025, including Clearview AI ($51.75M settlement). ([Privacy World](https://www.privacyworld.blog/2025/12/2025-year-in-review-biometric-privacy-litigation/))

### Overall Fines
- In 2025, Big Tech paid over **$3.5 billion** in AI governance fines and settlements for data scraping, biometric privacy violations, and algorithmic overreach. ([Substack - AI Governance Lead](https://aigovernancelead.substack.com/p/5-ai-governance-payouts-35b-in-fines))

---

## Summary of Key Implications

1. **No court has issued a final ruling on fair use in AI training.** The earliest expected is summer 2026. The split so far: legal acquisition + training = likely fair use; pirated acquisition = not fair use.

2. **AI conversations have no legal privilege.** The Heppner ruling establishes that chats with AI are discoverable, not protected by attorney-client privilege, and can be subpoenaed by law enforcement or opposing counsel.

3. **DeepSeek is effectively banned** from U.S. government devices at federal and multiple state levels, and blocked entirely in Italy.

4. **The OpenAI-Pentagon deal** represents a major policy fork -- Anthropic refused to allow military surveillance/weapons use; OpenAI accepted with contractual language that critics call insufficient.

5. **GDPR enforcement against AI companies is accelerating** -- OpenAI fined 15M EUR, Replika fined 5M EUR, DeepSeek banned in Italy, investigations open across multiple EU member states.

6. **State AI laws are proliferating** but face potential federal preemption under Trump's December 2025 executive order.

([National Law Review](https://natlawreview.com/article/openai-loses-privacy-gambit-20-million-chatgpt-logs-likely-headed-copyright), [Law.com](https://www.law.com/legaltechnews/2026/01/07/openai-must-turn-over-millions-of-chat-logs-in-copyright-litigation-ny-federal-judge-rules-/?slreturn=20260110090802), [Lawyer Monthly](https://www.lawyer-monthly.com/2026/01/openai-sdny-discovery-20m-chat-logs-legal-impact/))

- **No trial date set.** No final ruling on fair use has been issued. ([Medium - Patent AI Lab](https://medium.com/@patentailab/nyt-vs-openai-lawsuit-update-2026-did-regurgitation-kill-the-fair-use-defense-d63ff021b805))

---

## 2. Major AI Copyright / Training Data Cases

### Bartz v. Anthropic -- $1.5 Billion Settlement (N.D. Cal.)
- **June 2025:** Judge William Alsup ruled that using copyrighted books to train LLMs is **"transformative -- spectacularly so"** and constitutes fair use **if the works were legally acquired**. However, he denied Anthropic's summary judgment on the piracy angle, finding that downloading millions of pirated books from shadow libraries was **not** fair use. ([IPWatchdog](https://ipwatchdog.com/2025/12/23/copyright-ai-collide-three-key-decisions-ai-training-copyrighted-content-2025/))
- **August 2025:** Anthropic agreed to a historic **$1.5 billion settlement** -- roughly $3,000 per title for ~500,000 books. ([Copyright Alliance](https://copyrightalliance.org/participating-bartz-v-anthropic-settlement/), [Authors Guild](https://authorsguild.org/advocacy/artificial-intelligence/what-authors-need-to-know-about-the-anthropic-settlement/), [NPR](https://www.npr.org/2025/09/05/nx-s1-5529404/anthropic-settlement-authors-copyright-ai))
- **March 2026:** Claims deadline is March 30, 2026. Fairness hearing set for April 2026. Preliminary approval granted; final approval pending. ([Anthropic Settlement Site](https://www.anthropiccopyrightsettlement.com/))

### Kadrey v. Meta (N.D. Cal.)
- **June 2025:** Judge Chhabria found Meta's use of copyrighted works to train its LLM was **highly transformative and fair use**, though he noted transformation alone is not dispositive. ([Skadden](https://www.skadden.com/insights/publications/2025/07/fair-use-and-ai-training))

### Thomson Reuters v. ROSS Intelligence (D. Del. / 3rd Circuit)
- Trial court held training use was **not fair use**. Currently **on appeal** in the Third Circuit, where questions of copyrightability and fair use will be decided. ([Copyright Alliance](https://copyrightalliance.org/ai-copyright-lawsuit-developments-2025/))

### Andersen v. Stability AI, Midjourney, DeviantArt (N.D. Cal.)
- **August 2024:** Judge refused to dismiss core copyright claims, allowing discovery to proceed.
- **August 2025:** Midjourney filed a 43-page answer leaning on fair use and the First Amendment (no motion to dismiss).
- **Late 2025:** Parties in active document discovery. ([McKool Smith](https://www.mckoolsmith.com/newsroom-ailitigation-46), [NYU JIPEL](https://jipel.law.nyu.edu/andersen-v-stability-ai-the-landmark-case-unpacking-the-copyright-risks-of-ai-image-generators/))

### Disney/Universal/Warner Bros. v. Midjourney
- **June 2025:** Disney and Universal filed a complaint alleging Midjourney reproduces Marvel and Star Wars characters.
- **November 2025:** Warner Bros. case consolidated with Disney's. ([McKool Smith](https://www.mckoolsmith.com/newsroom-ailitigation-46))

### Reddit v. Anthropic (California state court)
- Reddit sued Anthropic for breach of contract, unjust enrichment, trespass to chattels, and unfair competition, alleging Anthropic deployed automated bots to scrape Reddit content over 100,000 times despite publicly claiming it does not scrape Reddit. ([Fortune](https://fortune.com/2026/02/24/anthropic-china-deepseek-theft-claude-distillation-copyright-national-security/))

### Music Publishers v. Anthropic (M.D. Tenn.)
- UMG, Concord, and others sued Anthropic in October 2023 for massive-scale music lyric copyright infringement via web scraping. Case ongoing. ([Copyright Alliance](https://copyrightalliance.org/ai-copyright-lawsuit-developments-2025/))

**Overall landscape:** As of October 2025, there were at least **51 copyright lawsuits** against AI companies. No court is expected to issue a final fair use ruling in AI training until **summer 2026 at the earliest**. ([Chat GPT Is Eating the World](https://chatgptiseatingtheworld.com/2025/10/08/status-of-all-51-copyright-lawsuits-v-ai-oct-8-2025-no-more-decisions-on-fair-use-in-2025/))

---

## 3. AI Chat History as Evidence -- New Precedents

### US v. Heppner (S.D.N.Y.) -- Landmark Privilege Ruling
- **February 10, 2026:** Judge Jed Rakoff ruled that **conversations between a criminal defendant and Anthropic's Claude are not protected** by attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine. Reasoning: Claude is not a lawyer, and "discussion of legal issues between two non-attorneys is not protected." The defendant had no reasonable expectation of confidentiality. ([HSF Kramer](https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/litigation/2026-02/new-york-court-finds-client-chats-with-generative-ai-tool-claude-are-not-privileged), [Crowell & Moring](https://www.crowell.com/en/insights/client-alerts/federal-court-rules-some-ai-chats-are-not-protected-by-legal-privilege-what-it-means-for-you))

### Criminal Cases
- From January to June 2025, OpenAI received **119 requests for user account information, 26 requests for chat content, and 1 emergency request** from law enforcement.
- Three 2025 criminal prosecutions cited ChatGPT conversations as evidence in cases involving **child exploitation, arson, and vandalism**. ([Kolmogorov Law](https://www.kolmogorovlaw.com/the-chatgpt-subpoena-revolution-when-your-ai-conversations-become-court-evidence), [Van Ho Law](https://www.vanholaw.com/blog/2026/february/when-chatbots-become-witnesses-how-ai-conversati/))

### FBI Seizure of CEO's AI Chats
- The FBI seized a CEO's computer and sought access to his AI chat history. Defense attorneys argued attorney-client privilege; the court left only a **narrow, untested opening** -- if an attorney specifically directed the client to conduct AI research as part of attorney work product. ([California Employment Law Report](https://www.californiaemploymentlawreport.com/2026/02/the-fbi-seized-a-ceos-ai-chats-and-four-more-reasons-california-employers-cant-ignore-ai-any-longer/))

### Divorce / Family Law
- California courts are grappling with AI-generated evidence in family law. By **July 2025**, all California courts serving populations over 500,000 must implement basic AI document review capabilities, with full statewide implementation by December 2026. ([Lass Law](https://www.lasslaw.com/ai-in-the-courtroom-how-californias-new-2025-rule-may-change-your-divorce-strategy/), [Dentons](https://www.dentons.com/en/insights/newsletters/2026/february/23/divorce-and-family-law-insights/what-happens-when-you-ask-ai-about-your-divorce))

### Immigration
- USCIS deployed an **Evidence Classifier** AI system that doubled the rate of 30-day application processing (30% to 58%) and saved 13,000+ hours. AI outputs (including chat messages, emails) are subject to mandatory human review before enforcement action. ([EB-5 Insights](https://www.eb5insights.com/2025/12/18/the-us-governments-expanding-use-of-ai-in-immigration-adjudications-considerations-for-employers-and-investors/))

### Key Takeaway
Sam Altman publicly warned in a 2025 podcast that people treating ChatGPT like a therapist or lawyer should understand those conversations **can be subpoenaed**. A survey found **half of AI users are unaware** their chats can be compelled as evidence. ([Bressler](https://www.bressler.com/publication-the-new-frontier-of-evidence-ai-chat-histories))

---

## 4. Regulatory Actions

### FTC Actions Against AI Companies
- **September 2025:** FTC launched a formal inquiry into **AI companion chatbots**, ordering 7 companies to provide information on impacts to children and youth. ([FTC](https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2025/09/ftc-launches-inquiry-ai-chatbots-acting-companions))
- **August 2025:** FTC approved final orders against **Workado LLC** (false "98% accuracy" claims for AI content detection -- actual accuracy ~53%) and filed suit against **Air AI Technologies**. ([Perkins Coie](https://perkinscoie.com/insights/blog/privacy-law-recap-2025-ftc-enforcement))
- **December 2025:** FTC reopened and set aside a 2024 consent order involving **Rytr LLC**, signaling a shift in enforcement approach. ([Fox Rothschild](https://dataprivacy.foxrothschild.com/2026/01/articles/general-privacy-data-security-news-developments/ftc-dismissal-of-settlement-with-ai-company-signals-shift-in-enforcement-focus/))

### Character.AI Teen Suicide Lawsuits -- Settlement
- Multiple lawsuits filed after **teen suicides** linked to Character.AI chatbots (Sewell Setzer III, age 14, Florida; Juliana Peralta, age 13, Colorado; a 17-year-old autistic teen in Texas).
- **January 2026:** Character.AI and Google agreed to **settle** the lawsuits. ([CNN](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/07/business/character-ai-google-settle-teen-suicide-lawsuit), [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/07/google-characterai-to-settle-suits-involving-suicides-ai-chatbots.html))
- Character.AI banned under-18 open-ended chats in late 2025, criticized as too late. ([TorHoerman Law](https://www.torhoermanlaw.com/ai-lawsuit/character-ai-lawsuit/))

### EU AI Act Enforcement
- **February 2, 2025:** Prohibited practices (Article 5) became enforceable.
- **August 2, 2025:** Full enforcement of prohibited practices began.
- **August 2, 2026:** High-risk AI system requirements (Annex III) become enforceable -- employment, credit, education, law enforcement AI.
- **No enforcement actions announced yet**, but investigations are reportedly underway into workplace emotion recognition systems, predictive policing algorithms, and social scoring in employee management. ([Secure Privacy](https://secureprivacy.ai/blog/eu-ai-act-2026-compliance), [Legal Nodes](https://www.legalnodes.com/article/eu-ai-act-2026-updates-compliance-requirements-and-business-risks))
- **Penalties:** Up to 35 million EUR or 7% of global turnover for prohibited AI violations.

### GDPR Enforcement Against AI Providers

**Italian DPA (Garante):**
- **December 2024:** Fined OpenAI **15 million EUR** for GDPR violations (no legal basis for training on personal data, failure to notify of March 2023 breach, no age verification). OpenAI is appealing. ([Euronews](https://www.euronews.com/next/2024/12/20/italys-privacy-watchdog-fines-openai-15-million-after-probe-into-chatgpt-data-collection))
- **April/May 2025:** Fined **Replika (Luka Inc.) 5 million EUR** for GDPR violations -- no valid legal basis for data processing, transparency failures, and failure to implement age verification despite risks to minors. ([EDPB](https://www.edpb.europa.eu/news/national-news/2025/ai-italian-supervisory-authority-fines-company-behind-chatbot-replika_en))
- **January 30, 2025:** **Banned DeepSeek** in Italy, imposing a definitive limitation on processing Italian users' personal data. DeepSeek claimed it didn't operate in Italy; Garante disagreed. DeepSeek's privacy policy disclosed data storage in China. ([Bird & Bird](https://www.twobirds.com/en/insights/2025/the-garante-imposes-a-definitive-limitation-on-the-processing-of-italian-users%E2%80%99-personal-data))

**Other EU DPAs on DeepSeek:**
- **Ireland:** DPC sent information request to DeepSeek (Jan 29, 2025).
- **Portugal:** DECO Proteste filed complaint (Jan 31, 2025).
- **Spain:** OCU filed complaint with AEPD (Feb 3, 2025).
- **Germany (Berlin):** Asked DeepSeek to voluntarily remove apps (May 6, 2025); sent notice-and-action to Apple/Google (June 27, 2025). ([Usercentrics](https://usercentrics.com/knowledge-hub/eu-regulators-scrutinize-deepseek-for-data-privacy-violations/), [MIAI](https://ai-regulation.com/deepseek-one-year-later-regulatory-storm-global-surge/))

### DeepSeek Bans (United States)
- **Federal:** "No DeepSeek on Government Devices Act" (HR 1121) introduced February 7, 2025 by Reps. Gottheimer and LaHood. Navy and Commerce Department banned it on government devices. 21 state AGs urged Congress to pass the bill. ([Gottheimer.house.gov](https://gottheimer.house.gov/posts/release-gottheimer-lahood-introduce-new-bipartisan-legislation-to-protect-americans-from-deepseek), [NBC News](https://www.nbcnews.com/business/business-news/us-lawmakers-move-ban-deepseek-government-devices-chinese-surveillance-rcna190965))
- **State-level:** Texas became the first state to ban DeepSeek on all state devices (Jan 31, 2025). New York, Tennessee, Virginia, and others followed. ([StateTech](https://statetechmagazine.com/article/2025/04/these-states-have-banned-deepseek), [National Law Review](https://natlawreview.com/article/three-states-ban-deepseek-use-state-devices-and-networks))
- **Security finding:** Third-party analysis found DeepSeek's app can capture login information and share it with China Mobile (state-owned). ([Conference Board](https://www.conference-board.org/research/CED-Newsletters-Alerts/state-and-federal-governments-deepseak-ban))

### State-Level AI Privacy Laws
- **California:** SB 53 (Transparency in Frontier AI Act) signed September 2025, effective January 1, 2026 -- requires disclosure of risk management protocols for frontier models (revenue >$500M). AB 2013 (GAI Training Data Transparency Act) also effective Jan 1, 2026. AI Transparency Act (SB 942) delayed to August 2, 2026. ([National Law Review](https://natlawreview.com/article/new-california-ai-laws-taking-effect-2026))
- **Texas:** RAIGA (Responsible AI Governance Act) effective January 1, 2026 -- prohibits AI systems that incite harm, infringe constitutional rights, produce deepfakes or CSAM. ([Miller Nash](https://www.millernash.com/industry-news/from-colorado-to-texas-how-states-are-rewriting-ai-laws))
- **Colorado:** Comprehensive AI Act coming online June 2026.
- **Illinois:** Employer AI disclosure requirements starting January 2026.
- **Federal preemption uncertainty:** Trump's December 11, 2025 executive order seeks to preempt state AI laws; enforceability TBD by courts. ([King & Spalding](https://www.kslaw.com/news-and-insights/new-state-ai-laws-are-effective-on-january-1-2026-but-a-new-executive-order-signals-disruption))

---

## 5. National Security Letters / FISA / Surveillance

### NSLs Served to AI Companies
- **No public reporting** has confirmed NSLs specifically served to AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). These letters come with gag orders, so absence of public information is expected. No AI company has published a warrant canary statement confirming or denying receipt of NSLs as of this writing.

### OpenAI Transparency Report (H1 2025)
- OpenAI published its government requests report for January-June 2025. Policy: requires subpoena for account info, valid warrant for content. 119 account info requests, 26 content requests, 1 emergency request received. ([OpenAI](https://cdn.openai.com/trust-and-transparency/report-2025h1-government-requests-for-user-data.pdf))
- OpenAI also operates a **monitoring system that scans all ChatGPT conversations** for potentially harmful content, escalating concerning conversations to human reviewers who can report to law enforcement. ([EFF](https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2025/12/ai-chatbot-companies-should-protect-your-conversations-bulk-surveillance))

### Anthropic Transparency
- Anthropic published a government requests report (latest publicly available covers Jan-June 2024). Anthropic processes requests in accordance with applicable law and may disclose in emergencies involving danger of death or serious physical injury. ([Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/transparency/platform-security))

### OpenAI-Pentagon Deal (February-March 2026)
- After the Trump administration **blacklisted Anthropic** from a Pentagon contract because Anthropic refused to allow its AI to be used for autonomous weapons or mass domestic surveillance, OpenAI announced a deal to provide AI for classified Pentagon networks.
- OpenAI stated three "red lines": no mass domestic surveillance, no autonomous weapons direction. But the EFF criticized the contract language as "weasel words" -- it does not give OpenAI a free-standing right to prohibit otherwise-lawful government use, only stating the Pentagon cannot use OpenAI tech to break existing laws. ([MIT Technology Review](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/03/02/1133850/openais-compromise-with-the-pentagon-is-what-anthropic-feared/), [EFF](https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2026/03/weasel-words-openais-pentagon-deal-wont-stop-ai-powered-surveillance), [NPR](https://www.npr.org/2026/02/27/nx-s1-5729118/trump-anthropic-pentagon-openai-ai-weapons-ban))
- Altman admitted the deal "looked opportunistic and sloppy." ([CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/03/openai-sam-altman-pentagon-deal-amended-surveillance-limits.html))
- **FISA Section 702** is due to expire April 2026 if not renewed, which will generate significant debate. ([Perkins Coie](https://perkinscoie.com/insights/blog/privacy-and-data-security-recap-2025-national-security))

---

## 6. Additional Notable Cases

### Anthropic v. DeepSeek, Moonshot, MiniMax (Distillation Accusations)
- **February 2026:** Anthropic publicly accused three Chinese AI labs of **industrial-scale distillation attacks** -- using ~24,000 fraudulent accounts to generate 16 million+ exchanges with Claude to extract capabilities for their own models. DeepSeek specifically used Claude to generate censorship-safe alternatives for politically sensitive queries. Not a lawsuit (yet) but a public accusation with potential legal ramifications. ([CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/24/anthropic-openai-china-firms-distillation-deepseek.html), [Fortune](https://fortune.com/2026/02/24/anthropic-china-deepseek-theft-claude-distillation-copyright-national-security/), [The Hacker News](https://thehackernews.com/2026/02/anthropic-says-chinese-ai-firms-used-16.html))

### Google Gemini Privacy Lawsuit (N.D. Cal.)
- **October 2025:** Class action filed alleging Google **activated Gemini AI features** across Gmail, Chat, and Meet without user consent, enabling AI to read private communications. Putative class of all U.S. Google account holders. ([National Law Review](https://natlawreview.com/article/silent-switch-new-lawsuit-alleges-google-uses-gemini-ai-secretly-read-gmail-chat))

### Google $425.7M Data Privacy Verdict
- **December 2025:** Federal jury awarded **$425.7 million** to ~98 million Google users who were misled about data collection opt-outs on mobile devices. ([For The People](https://www.forthepeople.com/blog/google-users-win-4257m-verdict-data-privacy-lawsuit/))

### Meta BIPA Litigation
- **2025-2026:** Multiple ongoing BIPA class actions against Meta for biometric data collection (voice, facial recognition) without consent. Illinois courts rejected Meta's California choice-of-law defense. Prior settlements total $700M+. ([DLA Piper](https://privacymatters.dlapiper.com/2026/02/illinois-law-trumps-metas-california-choice-of-law-provision-in-bipa-class-action/))
- **2025 BIPA landscape:** 107+ new BIPA class actions filed in Illinois in 2025, including Clearview AI ($51.75M settlement). ([Privacy World](https://www.privacyworld.blog/2025/12/2025-year-in-review-biometric-privacy-litigation/))

### Overall Fines
- In 2025, Big Tech paid over **$3.5 billion** in AI governance fines and settlements for data scraping, biometric privacy violations, and algorithmic overreach. ([Substack - AI Governance Lead](https://aigovernancelead.substack.com/p/5-ai-governance-payouts-35b-in-fines))

---

## Summary of Key Implications

1. **No court has issued a final ruling on fair use in AI training.** The earliest expected is summer 2026. The split so far: legal acquisition + training = likely fair use; pirated acquisition = not fair use.

2. **AI conversations have no legal privilege.** The Heppner ruling establishes that chats with AI are discoverable, not protected by attorney-client privilege, and can be subpoenaed by law enforcement or opposing counsel.

3. **DeepSeek is effectively banned** from U.S. government devices at federal and multiple state levels, and blocked entirely in Italy.

4. **The OpenAI-Pentagon deal** represents a major policy fork -- Anthropic refused to allow military surveillance/weapons use; OpenAI accepted with contractual language that critics call insufficient.

5. **GDPR enforcement against AI companies is accelerating** -- OpenAI fined 15M EUR, Replika fined 5M EUR, DeepSeek banned in Italy, investigations open across multiple EU member states.

6. **State AI laws are proliferating** but face potential federal preemption under Trump's December 2025 executive order.

   133→**Recent Court Rulings (2025):**
   134→
   135→1. **NYT v. OpenAI (June 2025):** Federal judge ordered OpenAI to preserve ALL ChatGPT user logs, including "temporary" chats and API requests, regardless of user opt-out settings[230][242]
   136→   - Result: "We do not retain your chats" promise overridden by court order
   137→   - All business logs (trade secrets, privileged data) must be preserved
   138→
   139→2. **Divorce/Custody Cases (2024-2025):** Multiple cases used ChatGPT/Claude history as evidence[224][227][233]
   140→   - Custody case: ChatGPT history questioned parent's mental fitness
   141→   - Workplace dispute: Private conversations proved harassment
   142→   - Divorce case: Spouse's AI chats subpoenaed for hidden assets
   143→
   144→3. **Legal Precedent:** Courts treat AI chat history like any digital evidence (texts, emails)[224][236]
   145→   - Admissible in court
   146→   - No attorney-client privilege for AI conversations
   147→   - Deletion after preservation notice = spoliation (sanctions)
   148→
   149→---
   150→
   151→### **OpenAI (ChatGPT / GPT-5.1) Privacy Analysis**
   152→
   153→#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**
   154→
   155→**ChatGPT Plus/Pro ($20-200/mo):**
   156→- **Default:** Chats saved for training unless you disable chat history
   157→- **"Temporary" chats:** Previously deleted after session, BUT NYT court order requires preservation regardless of settings[230][242]
   158→- **Opt-out:** Settings → Data Controls → "Improve the model for everyone" (toggle off)
   159→  - Effect: Data not used for training
   160→  - BUT: Still retained for 30 days minimum (compliance, abuse detection)
   161→  - AND: Court can still subpoena (retention override)
   162→
   163→**ChatGPT API / Business:**
   164→- **Zero data retention agreement (ZDR):** Available for Enterprise customers
   165→- **Default API:** 30-day retention for abuse/monitoring
   166→- **BUT:** NYT ruling suggests courts can compel preservation regardless of ZDR
   167→
   168→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   169→
   170→**How to Opt Out:**
   171→1. Go to Settings → Data Controls
   172→2. Toggle OFF: "Improve the model for everyone"
   173→3. Toggle ON: "Temporary Chat" (but see court order caveat)
   174→
   175→**Effect:**
   176→- ✅ Your chats won't train GPT-6
   177→- ⚠️ Still retained 30 days (abuse monitoring)
   178→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   183→
   184→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   185→|----------|-----------|-----|
   186→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | FBI can serve NSL directly to OpenAI; gag order prevents disclosure; full chat history accessible |
   187→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Texas/AZ AG can subpoena OpenAI (US company, US jurisdiction); immediate compliance likely |
   188→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | OpenAI is US-based; cross-border transfer concerns; GDPR right to deletion may conflict with US court orders |
   189→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Multiple precedents; courts routinely subpoena ChatGPT history[227][233][236] |
   190→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer can request logs if using company API keys; individual use can be subpoenaed in disputes |
   191→
   192→**Key Risk:** OpenAI is **US-based (California)**, subject to US courts, Five Eyes intelligence sharing, and NSL/FISA court orders[224][230][242].
   193→
   194→---
   195→
   196→### **Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.5 / Sonnet 4.5) Privacy Analysis**
   197→
   198→#### **Data Retention Policy (Updated Sept 28, 2025)**
   199→
   200→**MAJOR POLICY CHANGE (Sept 2025):**
   201→- **Previous:** 30-day retention (all users)
   202→- **New (as of Sept 28, 2025):** **5-year retention** if you allow training[225][228][234][237][240]
   203→
   204→**Consumer Accounts (Free, Pro, Max):**
   205→- **Default:** "Help improve Claude" toggle = **ON** (opt-in to training)
   206→- **If enabled:** 5-year retention, data used for model training
   207→- **If disabled:** 30-day retention, no training use
   208→- **Critical:** Team/Max users ($18-100+/mo) are treated as "consumers" unless on Commercial Terms[225]
   209→
   210→**Business/Enterprise (Claude for Work, API):**
   211→- **Commercial Terms:** No training, minimal retention, GDPR-compliant
   212→- **Zero Data Retention (ZDR):** Available for API users (but see caveat below)
   213→- **BUT:** ZDR doesn't cover Files API exception[228]
   214→
   215→#### **The 5-Year Retention Controversy**
   216→
   217→**Why This Matters:**
   218→- **6,000% increase** from 30 days to 5 years (1,825 days)[225]
   219→- Your 2025 conversations could train Claude models in 2030
   220→- Law firms, consultants, healthcare providers potentially violating compliance (HIPAA, attorney-client privilege)[225]
   221→- **No middle option:** Either allow training + 5 years, OR disable training + lose personalization features[231]
   222→
   223→**Affected Users:**
   224→- ✅ All Claude Free users
   225→- ✅ All Claude Pro users ($20/mo)
   226→- ✅ All Claude Max users ($100+/mo)
   227→- ✅ All Claude Team users (unless upgraded to Enterprise)
   228→- ❌ Claude for Work / Enterprise (Commercial Terms apply)
   229→- ❌ API users with ZDR agreement
   230→
   231→---
   232→
   233→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   234→
   235→**How to Opt Out:**
   236→1. Go to Settings → Privacy Settings
   237→2. Toggle OFF: "Help improve Claude"
   238→3. File Data Processing Objection (optional, for formal record)
   239→
   240→**Effect:**
   241→- ✅ Data not used for training
   242→- ✅ Retention reduced to 30 days
   243→- ❌ Lose personalization/memory features
   244→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
   245→
   246→**Trust & Safety Exception:**
   247→- Content flagged for abuse retained up to **2 years** (inputs/outputs)
   248→- Classification scores retained up to **7 years**
   249→- Applies even if training disabled[228]
   250→
   251→---
   252→
   253→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   254→
   255→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   256→|----------|-----------|-----|
   257→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Anthropic is US-based (California); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; 5-year retention = extended exposure |
   258→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction, immediate compliance likely; 5-year retention means old conversations still accessible |
   259→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | US-based company, cross-border transfer concerns; GDPR deletion rights may conflict with 5-year retention |
   260→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | No precedent yet, but same risk as ChatGPT; courts will treat Claude history like ChatGPT[224][227][239] |
   261→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer API keys = employer can request logs; individual use can be subpoenaed |
   262→
   263→**Key Risk:** Anthropic is **US-based (San Francisco)**, subject to same legal framework as OpenAI[225][234][237][239].
   264→
   265→**Additional Privacy Concern:** 5-year retention policy means conversations from 2020 still accessible in 2025 (if you had training enabled)[225][240].
   266→
   267→---
   268→
   269→### **Google (Gemini 3.0 Pro) Privacy Analysis**
   270→
   271→#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**
   272→
   273→**Gemini App (Free, Plus, Pro, Ultra):**
   274→- **Default:** Conversations saved for training unless you disable
   275→- **Opt-out:** Settings → Gemini Apps Activity → "Save Gemini Apps Activity" (toggle off)
   276→  - Effect: Conversations not saved, not used for training
   277→  - BUT: Google can still log metadata (timestamps, query types) for abuse detection
   278→
   279→**Gemini API / Vertex AI (Business):**
   280→- **ZDR available:** API users can opt out of data retention
   281→- **Grounding with Google Search:** Billing starts Jan 5, 2026 (previously free)[207]
   282→
   283→**Google Workspace (Business):**
   284→- **Smart features disabled by default:** Admins must enable for AI training
   285→- **Can opt out:** Settings → "Use my data for AI improvement" (toggle off)
   286→
   287→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   288→
   289→**How to Opt Out (Gemini App):**
   290→1. Go to Gemini Apps Activity settings
   291→2. Toggle OFF: "Save Gemini Apps Activity"
   292→3. Delete existing history (optional)
   293→
   294→**How to Opt Out (Workspace):**
   295→1. Admin console → Settings → Smart features
   296→2. Disable: "Use data for AI training"
   297→
   298→**Effect:**
   299→- ✅ Conversations not used for training
   300→- ⚠️ Metadata still logged (abuse/compliance)
   301→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
   302→
   303→---
   304→
   305→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   306→
   307→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   308→|----------|-----------|-----|
   309→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Google is US-based (California); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; extensive data retention infrastructure |
   310→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction; Google has cooperated with law enforcement in past; immediate compliance likely |
   311→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **MODERATE** | Google has EU data centers, GDPR-compliant infrastructure; better than OpenAI/Anthropic but still US parent company |
   312→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Same risk as ChatGPT/Claude; courts will subpoena Gemini history |
   313→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Google Workspace admins can access user logs; individual Gemini use can be subpoenaed |
   314→
   315→**Key Risk:** Google is **US-based (California)**, but has **extensive EU infrastructure** (Gemini available via Vertex AI in EU regions)[213][216].
   316→
   317→**Advantage over OpenAI/Anthropic:** Google has EU data centers, can route EU traffic through EU-only infrastructure for better GDPR compliance.
   318→
   319→---
   320→
   321→### **DeepSeek (V3.2) Privacy Analysis**
   322→
   323→#### **Data Retention Policy (Dec 2025)**
   324→
   325→**DeepSeek API:**
   326→- **China-based company:** Subject to Chinese data protection laws
   327→- **Data retention:** Not clearly disclosed (no public privacy policy in English as of Dec 2025)
   328→- **Training use:** Assumed data used for training (standard for Chinese AI companies)
   329→
   330→**Self-Hosted (Open-Source):**
   331→- **You control data:** Run on your own servers
   332→- **No external transmission:** Data never leaves your infrastructure
   333→- **Zero retention risk:** No company can be subpoenaed if you self-host
   334→
   335→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   336→
   337→**DeepSeek API:**
   338→- **No opt-out disclosed:** Assume all data used for training
   339→- **Recommendation:** DO NOT use DeepSeek API for sensitive data
   340→
   341→**Self-Hosted:**
   342→- **N/A:** You control all data
   343→
   344→---
   345→
   346→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   347→
   348→| Scenario | Risk Level | Explanation |
   349→|----------|-----------|-------------|
   350→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | ⚠️ **COMPLICATED** | China-based company; US courts have no jurisdiction; BUT Chinese gov't can access data under China's National Security Law |
   351→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🟢 **LOW** | US courts cannot compel Chinese company; Texas AG has no jurisdiction |
   352→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | ⚠️ **COMPLICATED** | No EU presence; GDPR doesn't apply; but Chinese data protection laws may apply |
   353→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🟢 **LOW** | US courts unlikely to successfully subpoena Chinese company |
   354→| **Chinese Gov't Access** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Chinese National Security Law requires companies to provide data to gov't on request (no judicial oversight) |
   355→
   356→**Key Risk:** DeepSeek is **China-based (Beijing)**, subject to Chinese government data access laws[177][183][186][192].
   357→
   358→**Recommendation for Vulnerable Communities:** **DO NOT use DeepSeek API** if serving populations at risk from Chinese government (Uyghurs, Hong Kong activists, Taiwan-related orgs, etc.).
   359→
   360→**Safe Alternative:** **Self-host DeepSeek** (open-source weights) on your own servers (Iceland, Switzerland) → zero external data transmission.
   361→
   362→---
   363→
   364→## PART 3: PRIVACY SCORECARD & RECOMMENDATIONS
   365→
   366→### **Privacy Risk Matrix (December 2025)**
   367→
   368→| Provider | Model | US Jurisdiction | Data Retention | Training Opt-Out | Subpoena Risk | Court Precedent | **Overall Risk** |
   369→|----------|-------|----------------|----------------|-----------------|---------------|----------------|------------------|
   370→| **OpenAI** | GPT-5.1 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | 30+ days | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ✅ Yes (NYT case) | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
   371→| **Anthropic** | Opus 4.5 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | 5 years (!) | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ⚠️ Likely same | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
   372→| **Google** | Gemini 3.0 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | Varies | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ⚠️ Likely same | **🔴 HIGH** |
   373→| **DeepSeek** | V3.2 (API) | 🟡 China | Unknown | ❌ None | ⚠️ China gov't | ❌ None | **🔴 HIGH** |
   374→| **DeepSeek** | V3.2 (Self-Host) | ✅ Your choice | ✅ You control | ✅ N/A | 🟢 None | ❌ None | **🟢 LOWEST** |
   375→
   376→---
   377→
   378→### **RECOMMENDATIONS FOR COMMON CLOUD DEPLOYMENT**
   379→
   380→#### **For MVP (Months 1-6): Use API with Maximum Privacy Settings**
   381→
   382→**Option 1: Claude Opus 4.5 API (Recommended)**
   383→- ✅ Commercial Terms (no training, minimal retention)
   384→- ✅ Best coding performance (Terminal Bench leader)
   385→- ✅ Extended thinking (preserves reasoning across conversations)
   386→- ❌ US jurisdiction (NSL risk)
   387→- ❌ 5-year retention if using consumer account
   388→
   389→**Setup:**
   390→```
   391→1. Sign up for Claude API (Commercial Terms, NOT consumer)
   392→2. Disable all training/logging: API settings → "Zero Data Retention"
   393→3. Use API keys only (never Claude.ai consumer interface)
   394→4. Rotate API keys monthly (minimize exposure window)
   395→5. Log all prompts locally (your infrastructure, encrypted)
   396→6. Never use consumer Claude.ai for deployment work
   397→```
   398→
   399→**Cost:** ~$3-15/M tokens (Opus 4.5 pricing TBD, likely competitive with GPT-5)
   400→
   401→---
   402→
   403→**Option 2: Self-Hosted DeepSeek-V3.2 (Maximum Privacy)**
   404→- ✅ You control all data (Iceland/Switzerland servers)
   405→- ✅ Zero external transmission
   406→- ✅ No company can be subpoenaed
   407→- ✅ Open-source (auditable)
   408→- ❌ Requires significant compute (8x A100 GPUs or equivalent)
   409→- ❌ Complex setup (not viable for solo operator)
   410→
   411→**Setup:**
   412→```
   413→1. Provision GPU server in Iceland (Hosting.is) or Switzerland (Infomaniak)
   414→2. Download DeepSeek-V3.2 weights (HuggingFace)
   415→3. Install vLLM or TGI (inference server)
   416→4. Deploy n8n → calls local DeepSeek instance
   417→5. All data stays on your servers
   418→6. No external API calls = zero subpoena risk
   419→```
   420→
   421→**Cost:** 
   422→- GPU server: €500-1000/mo (8x A100 equivalent)
   423→- One-time setup: ~€5,000 (engineer time)
   424→- **NOT RECOMMENDED for MVP** (too expensive/complex)
   425→
   426→---
   427→
   428→**Option 3: Gemini 3.0 Pro API via Vertex AI (EU-Compliant Compromise)**
   429→- ✅ EU data centers (Germany, Netherlands)
   430→- ✅ GDPR-compliant infrastructure
   431→- ⚠️ Still US parent company (Google)
   432→- ⚠️ Can be subpoenaed by US courts (but harder than OpenAI/Anthropic)
   433→
   434→**Setup:**
   435→```
   436→1. Sign up for Google Cloud (Vertex AI)
   437→2. Select EU region (europe-west1 = Belgium, europe-west3 = Frankfurt)
   438→3. Enable Gemini 3.0 Pro API
   439→4. Configure: Data residency = EU only
   440→5. Use Vertex AI API (NOT Gemini.ai consumer interface)
   441→6. Rotate API keys monthly
   442→```
   443→
   444→**Cost:** ~$7/M tokens (slightly higher than Claude/GPT)
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→### **CRITICAL: NEVER Use Consumer Interfaces for Deployment**
   449→
   450→**❌ DO NOT USE:**
   451→- ChatGPT.com (consumer interface)
   452→- Claude.ai (consumer interface)
   453→- Gemini app (consumer interface)
   454→
   455→**✅ ALWAYS USE:**
   456→- OpenAI API (with ZDR agreement)
   457→- Claude API (Commercial Terms)
   458→- Vertex AI (Gemini API)
   459→
   460→**Why:** Consumer interfaces have **no privacy guarantees**, **5-year retention** (Claude), and **can be subpoenaed** (NYT precedent)[225][230][234][242].
   461→
   462→---
   463→
   464→## PART 4: UPDATED AI TOOLCHAIN (DECEMBER 2025)
   465→
   466→### **The Privacy-First Stack**
   467→
   468→```
   469→PLANNING & ARCHITECTURE (10% human, 90% AI)
   470→├─ Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)
   471→│   └─ Prompt: "Design 3-region DBA architecture"
   472→│   └─ Output: 50-page spec with Terraform modules
   473→│   └─ Privacy: Commercial Terms, minimal retention, no training
   474→│
   475→CODING & CONFIGURATION (5% human, 95% AI)
   476→├─ Cursor AI (with Claude Opus 4.5 API)
   477→│   └─ Configure: Use API key (not Claude.ai consumer)
   478→│   └─ Multi-file editing: Ansible playbooks, Docker configs
   479→│   └─ Privacy: All data via API (Commercial Terms)
   480→│
   481→WORKFLOW ORCHESTRATION (0% human, 100% AI)
   482→├─ n8n AI Workflow Builder
   483→│   └─ Use Gemini 3.0 Pro (Vertex AI, EU region)
   484→│   └─ OR: Self-hosted DeepSeek-V3.2 (if budget allows)
   485→│   └─ Privacy: EU data residency OR self-hosted
   486→│
   487→TESTING & DEBUGGING (20% human, 80% AI)
   488→├─ OpenAI o3-mini (API with ZDR)
   489→│   └─ OR: Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)
   490→│   └─ Privacy: ZDR agreement, minimal retention
   491→│
   492→MONITORING & INCIDENT RESPONSE (10% human, 90% AI)
   493→├─ n8n + AI Agent monitoring workflows
   494→│   └─ Use Gemini 3.0 Pro (Vertex AI, EU region)
   495→│   └─ Privacy: EU data residency, no US API calls
   496→│
   497→DOCUMENTATION (0% human, 100% AI)
   498→├─ Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)
   499→│   └─ Generates deployment docs, runbooks, API refs
   500→│   └─ Privacy: Commercial Terms, no training
   501→```
   502→
   503→---
   504→
   505→## PART 5: COST COMPARISON (UPDATED DECEMBER 2025)
   506→
   507→### **API Costs (per 1M tokens)**
   508→
   509→| Model | Input Cost | Output Cost | Total (avg prompt) | Privacy Level |
   510→|-------|-----------|-------------|-------------------|---------------|
   511→| **Claude Opus 4.5** | TBD | TBD | ~$10-20 (est.) | Commercial Terms ✅ |
   512→| **Claude Sonnet 4.5** | $3 | $15 | ~$9 | Commercial Terms ✅ |
   513→| **GPT-5.1** | ~$10-15 | ~$30-50 | ~$20-32 | ZDR available ✅ |
   514→| **GPT-5.1-Codex-Max** | TBD | TBD | ~$25-40 (est.) | ZDR available ✅ |
   515→| **Gemini 3.0 Pro** | ~$7 | ~$21 | ~$14 | EU residency ✅ |
   516→| **DeepSeek-V3.2 (API)** | $0.028 | $1.10 | ~$0.56 | ⚠️ China jurisdiction |
   517→| **DeepSeek-V3.2 (Self)** | $0 | $0 | **€500-1000/mo GPU** | ✅ Full control |
   518→
   519→---
   520→
   521→### **Deployment Cost Estimate (6 Weeks)**
   522→
   523→| Task | Claude Opus 4.5 API | GPT-5.1-Codex-Max | Gemini 3.0 Pro (Vertex) | DeepSeek Self-Host |
   524→|------|-------------------|------------------|------------------------|-------------------|
   525→| Architecture (50K tokens) | ~$50 | ~$120 | ~$70 | ~€500/mo |
   526→| Terraform gen (200K tokens) | ~$200 | ~$480 | ~$280 | (same) |
   527→| Ansible gen (150K tokens) | ~$150 | ~$360 | ~$210 | (same) |
   528→| n8n workflows (100K tokens) | ~$100 | ~$240 | ~$140 | (same) |
   529→| Testing (300K tokens) | ~$300 | ~$720 | ~$420 | (same) |
   530→| Docs (50K tokens) | ~$50 | ~$120 | ~$70 | (same) |
   531→| **TOTAL (6 weeks)** | **~$850** | **~$2,040** | **~$1,190** | **~€3,000-6,000** |
   532→
   533→**Recommendation:** **Claude Opus 4.5 API** (Commercial Terms) = best privacy/cost/performance balance.
   534→
   535→---
   536→
   537→## PART 6: FINAL RECOMMENDATIONS
   538→
   539→### **For Common Cloud Deployment (Vulnerable Communities)**
   540→
   541→**Phase 1 (MVP, Months 1-6):**
   542→- ✅ **Use: Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms)**
   543→  - Best coding performance
   544→  - Commercial Terms = no training, minimal retention
   545→  - US jurisdiction (NSL risk) but acceptable for MVP
   546→  - Cost: ~$850 for 6-week deployment
   547→
   548→- ⚠️ **Avoid: Consumer interfaces** (ChatGPT.com, Claude.ai, Gemini app)
   549→  - 5-year retention (Claude)
   550→  - Can be subpoenaed (NYT precedent)
   551→  - No privacy guarantees
   552→
   553→- ⚠️ **Avoid: DeepSeek API**
   554→  - China jurisdiction
   555→  - Risk to Uyghur, Hong Kong, Taiwan-related orgs
   556→
   557→---
   558→
   559→**Phase 2 (Scale, Months 6-12):**
   560→- ✅ **Add: Gemini 3.0 Pro (Vertex AI, EU region)**
   561→  - EU data residency
   562→  - GDPR-compliant
   563→  - Better jurisdiction than US-only APIs
   564→  - Cost: ~$1,190 for 6-week deployment
   565→
   566→- ✅ **Consider: Self-hosted DeepSeek-V3.2** (if budget allows)
   567→  - Deploy on Iceland/Switzerland servers
   568→  - Zero external data transmission
   569→  - No subpoena risk
   570→  - Cost: €500-1000/mo GPU server (ongoing)
   571→
   572→---
   573→
   574→**Phase 3 (Mature, Year 2+):**
   575→- ✅ **Migrate to: Self-hosted open-source models**
   576→  - Llama 4 (Meta, open-source)
   577→  - DeepSeek-V3.2 (open-source)
   578→  - Mistral Large 3 (open-source)
   579→  - Deploy on Iceland/Switzerland servers
   580→  - Zero external API calls = maximum privacy
   581→
   582→---
   583→
   584→## CONCLUSION
   585→
   586→**December 2025 Reality:**
   587→- ALL major AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) are US-based, subject to NSLs, FISA courts, and subpoenas[224][225][230][234][239][242]
   588→- Court precedent (NYT v. OpenAI) shows retention promises can be overridden[230][242]
   589→- 5-year retention policy (Claude) creates extended exposure window[225][234][240]
   590→- Consumer interfaces (ChatGPT.com, Claude.ai) have NO privacy guarantees
   591→
   592→**Best Practice for Vulnerable Communities:**
   593→1. **Use API with Commercial Terms** (not consumer interfaces)
   594→2. **Rotate API keys monthly** (minimize exposure window)
   595→3. **Log all prompts locally** (your encrypted infrastructure)
   596→4. **Plan migration to self-hosted** (Year 2+, Iceland/Switzerland)
   597→5. **Never use AI for:**
   598→   - Real names of vulnerable individuals
   599→   - Immigration status information
   600→   - Legal strategy discussions (no attorney-client privilege)
   601→   - Medical records (HIPAA violations)
   602→   - Financial data (PCI-DSS violations)
   603→
   604→**Nicholas Merrill's Legacy:** "You can't encrypt your way out of bad infrastructure. You have to design privacy into the infrastructure itself."
   605→
   606→**For Common Cloud:** Start with Claude Opus 4.5 API (Commercial Terms) for speed, plan migration to self-hosted DeepSeek-V3.2 on Iceland servers for maximum sovereignty.
   607→
   608→---
   609→
   610→**End of Document**
   611→
   612→*Corrected analysis accounting for actual December 2025 models and privacy policies. Apologies for initial error.*
   613→
